(166:1.4) (175:1.9) Trust in the spirit within you to guide your words when enemies bring you before the rulers of synagogues and high authorities. (165:3.7)

name: Syracuse
description: During Jesus' week at Syracuse, he rehabilitated Ezra, the backslidden Jew, helping him find God and ultimately leading to the establishment of the first Christian church in the city. (130:8.2)

name: Syria
description: The Syrians, worshiping their gods, also believed in Yahweh of the Hebrews as mentioned by their prophets. (96:1.13) The broad-headed Nodite-Andonites in Syria introduced pottery, agriculture, and advanced metalworking techniques to the region. (80:1.2) Syria was once the center of early metalworking and pottery industry, as well as an important hub for agriculture and domesticated animal trade. (80:1.2) Syria thrived in peace and prosperity during the time of Jesus under Roman rule. (121:1.9) King David laid heavy tribute on the neighboring tribes, including the Syrians. (97:9.14) Malach from Syria welcomed Jesus in Beirut. (156:3.1) Matthew preached in Syria, Cappadocia, Galatia, Bithynia, and Thrace, ultimately dying triumphant in the faith. (139:7.10) Norana from Syria displayed unwavering faith in Jesus as a healer, leading to her daughter's miraculous healing and demonstrating the power of gentile belief in the teachings of the kingdom of heaven. (156:1) Pilate, serving under the legatus of Syria, ordered to Rome after condemning Jesus to crucifixion. (185:0.1) (185:1.6) Rome strategically played off Seleucid Syria against Ptolemaic Egypt to maintain control over the vital Palestinian highway and caravan routes, allowing an independent Palestine to thrive. (121:2.8) The western Nodite headquarters were established in northeastern Syria on the shores of the Mediterranean. (73:1.5)

name: system
description: The personality as the meaning of the whole signifies the unification and coordination of all factors of reality within a system of interconnected individual members. (112:1.17,19) The arrangement of different morontia and spirit beings creates majestic melodies. (44:1.7)

name: system co-ordinators
description: System Co-ordinators appear in Uversa personality registers as part of the Morontia Power Supervisors, a group inside the Universe Power Directors. (30:2.110) System co-ordinators harmonize differing power systems on morontia worlds to create a working unit for ascending mortals progressing from the physical to the spiritual. (48:2.15-16)

name: System Sovereigns
description: System Sovereigns, appointed in pairs or trios by the Constellation Father, lead the governments of individual systems for millennia, with transitions occurring only in the event of tragedy. (35:9.2) System Sovereigns are selected by a supreme council and appointed by the Constellation Father to rule in commissions of two or three on each system's headquarters, with changes in leadership occurring every dekamillennium unless a tragedy necessitates a sudden shift in personnel. (35:9.2-4) Chiefs serve for dekamillennium in commissions of two or three on the headquarters of System Sovereigns overseeing inhabited worlds. (35:9.2) (35:10.1) 709,841 primary Lanonandeks designated as System Sovereigns in Nebadon are assistants to the supreme councils of constellations and counselors in higher administrative work of the universe, entrusted with large executive responsibilities and unusual personal prerogatives. (20:1.10) (35:8.4) (45:2.1) Three System Sovereigns have rebelled in Nebadon. (35:9.5-6) (119:2.1-6) System Sovereigns, entrusted with wide powers of personal discretion, have exhibited disloyalty only three times in the history of Nebadon. (45:2.1) The Urantia government is advised by the returning governor general as a temporary member of the System Sovereign's cabinet of Planetary Princes. (114:3.4) The house of System Sovereigns presided over by the Most Highs functions directly with the superuniverse government once constellations are settled in light, leading to a shift in legislative functions. (55:9.2) System Sovereigns, not supervised by Trinity observers, exercise almost complete sovereignty over the local affairs of inhabited worlds. (35:9.5) Rarely do System Sovereigns grant permission for narration of another planet's affairs, making the presentation of Uranita's history a rare and extraordinary occurrence. (72:0.2) functions

name: Taanach
description: From the summit of the hill of Simeon at Nazareth, Jesus gazed upon Taanach, where Deborah and Barak defeated Sisera. (126:1.2)

name: Tabamantia
description: Tabamantia delivered a profound acknowledgment to the chief of Urantia Adjusters, expressing admiration and respect for the magnificent group of celestial ministers who have volunteered to serve on this irregular sphere. (108:3.5-6) Tabamantia, an agondonter of finaliter status, triumphed over insuperable difficulties and persevered through isolation from an early rebellion, proving the power of evolutionary will. (50:7.2) Tabamantia, the veteran finaliter and sovereign supervisor of Nebadon decimal planets, recommended the granting of Material Sons to Urantia after inspecting the planet and its progress. (49:5.12) (73:0.3) (108:3.5)

name: taboos
description: The seven commandments of Dalamatia and Eden, along with the ten injunctions of the Hebrews, were transformative taboos that replaced many pre-existing prohibitions and offered rewards for obedience. (89:1.4) Taboos served as the earliest form of societal regulation, evolving to become the ancestor of primitive self-control and foundational to ceremonial standards. (89:1.1) Sickness is believed to be a punishment for sin or taboo violation, a prevailing theory among various cultures throughout history. (90:3.8) Taboos on killing, believed to have been handed down from gods, were established by self-interest, sanctified by society as traditional mores, and consecrated by religion as moral law, resulting in the safety and sanctity of human life. (70:11.5) Taboos, burdensome yet effective in organizing society, serve as the foundation for the traditions and conventions that shape civilized man's established institutions. (69:0.3) (89:1.6) Consecrated land, marked with family insignia and protected by magic taboos, bestowed a “priest's title” to the owners, as seen with the Hebrews' respect for family landmarks and stone markers. (69:9.13) Establishing institutions of survival, such as industry, property, and war for gain, is rooted in the fear instinct and regulated by taboo, convention, and religious sanction. (69:1.3) Primitive society operated negatively through taboos, inherently restricting liberties and enforcing prohibitive laws. (70:11.2) Jesus delivered us from the slavery of taboo and taught us to see the world as fundamentally good. (196:2.9) The violation of taboos among early tribes was seen as both a crime and a sin, leading to punishment and the invention of hells for violators. (89:2.4) Taboos were man's earliest effort to avoid offending ghosts, evolving into ceremonial standards and primitive self-control. (89:1.1) Primitive religion upholds far-flung taboos essential for civilized society's evolution. (89:1.7) relating to taboos

name: tact
description: Tact is essential for success in securing the support and cooperation of others. (160:1.9) Tact is the fulcrum of social leverage, enabling wise souls to avoid unnecessary misunderstandings and navigate troublesome interactions with tolerance and grace. (156:5.18) The master gains victory gracefully through tact and tolerance, avoiding unnecessary battles. (160:3.4) Jesus, had it not been for a tactical blunder, would have managed the crowd and disarmed even his violent enemies with good-naturedness. (150:9.3)

name: tactics
description: Jesus never resorted to ignoble tactics in facing his adversaries, always responding with enlightening and dignified answers. (149:4.5) (173:2.7) Jesus would not compromise or serve evil to gain worship, choosing instead to abide by the Father's will. (136:9.3) Women, being weaker, have become more tactful by trading upon their sex charms and outgeneraled even the most primitive of men at home. (84:3.5) The way you live your life is more important than the work you do. (39:4.13)

name: tadpoles
description: Live loyally as a tadpole to become a growing and inspiring frog. (100:1.4) Frogs lay eggs in water, where their young first exist as tadpoles before undergoing metamorphosis. (59:5.6)

name: talents
description: money | pounds skills | achievement

name: Talmai
description: David sought to build himself up politically by marrying Saul's daughter, Nabal's widow, and Talmai's daughter, the king of Geshur. (97:9.10)

name: Tamar
description: Mary, the earth mother of Jesus, descended from a long line of remarkable women in the racial history of Urantia, including Annon, Tamar, Ruth, Bathsheba, Ansie, Cloa, Eve, Enta, and Ratta. (122:1.2)

name: Taoism
description: Taoism greatly influenced Buddhism in China, Shinto in Japan, and Christianity in Tibet, leading to its decline and transformation in various parts of the Orient over a millennium. (94:9.3) The earliest form of Taoism arose in China as a result of the teachings of Singlangton, Salem, and Indian philosophers, influencing a vastly different religion from modern Taoism. (94:5.2-6) Buddhism absorbed disintegrating Taoism's lingering ritualistic practices in China during the first millennium after Christ, creating a new, widely accepted synthetic religion. (94:11.1) Ganid’s abstract of Taoism emphasizes the pure, tranquil, and powerful nature of the Supreme Being, guiding humanity towards enlightenment and wisdom through humility and service. (131:8) Lao-tse envisioned God in Tao, alongside other great religious teachers like Gautama, Confucius, and Zoroaster, who arose in the sixth century before Christ. (92:5.12) A Mongolian merchant believer in Taoism spread the teachings of the universal Deity to his family and community. (130:2.2)

name: tapirs
description: Tapirs, along with a variety of other primitive mammals, roamed the forested mountain regions 45,000,000 years ago. (61:1.9) (61:7.15)

name: Tarentum
description: Jesus restrained bully attacking small lad at Tarentum, showing mercy over justice. (133:1.1) A man attacking his wife at the ship landing was gently approached by Jesus, who reminded him of the importance of mutual respect and equal partnership in marriage. (133:2)

name: Tarichea
description: Thomas the fisherman and Judas Iscariot met Jesus and the apostles at the fisher-boat landing at Tarichea. (138:5) (139:12.2) When Jesus first addressed the apostles, they did not suspect who he was due to their frequent encounters with the fish merchants of Tarichea. (192:1.9) Thomas, a former carpenter and stone mason turned fisherman in Tarichea on the west bank of the Sea of Galilee at Jordan outflow, was the analytical mind among the apostles. (139:8.2) Thomas resided at Tarichea, where, despite his lack of education, he was regarded as the leading citizen and possessed a keen, analytical mind. (139:8.2)

name: Tarim basin
description: The Tarim basin in Sinkiang was the eastern outpost of the Andite culture, where they built settlements and engaged in trade with the Chinese and Andonites. (79:1.2,8) The Tibetan centers were not as advanced as those of the Tarim basin, which was more advanced than Tibet. (79:6.5)

name: Tarshish
description: The young Philistine pointed out the ship landing of Jonah's voyage to Tarshish to Jesus, who taught him the importance of not escaping duty. (130:1.2)

name: Tarsus
description: Mithraism was dominant religion in Tarsus during Paul’s adolescence (98:7.9)

name: Tasmania
description: Andonic migration to Tasmania spanned from Europe to Java. (64:1.6)

name: taste designers
description: Celestial artisans, taste designers improve morontia taste and sharpen evolving spirit senses to enhance beauty appreciation. (44:6.8)

name: tattoo
description: The tattoo, an ancient badge of tribal membership, symbolized the hardships and endurance required for initiation ceremonies. (70:7.10) Tattooing is an artistic evolution of earlier crude scarring practices. (89:8.3)

name: tax collectors
description: Early rulers used secret clubs as a means for collecting taxes and debts, a practice that evolved from the tithe and temple offerings. (70:7.18) John taught his disciples to live by the spirit, not just the letter, of the law, instructing tax collectors to “extort no more than assigned” and soldiers to be content with their wages. (135:6.8) Jesus sold his harp and gave away the Greek Scriptures to avoid seizure by tax collectors and pay for his brother Jude's schooling. (126:5.5-6) (127:4.10) Matthew Levi was a publican who followed Jesus and dedicated his life to showing that God is no respecter of persons. (138:3.1,4) (181:2.14) Peter paid the temple tax for all three, fulfilling his promise to the temple-tax collector by catching a fish with a shekel in its mouth. (157:1) Zaccheus was a chief publican who much desired to see Jesus, and when the Master passed by the customs house in Jericho, he climbed up a sycamore tree to get a good view. (171:6.1)

name: taxation
description: Taxation enslavement of the citizenry by the state. (70:12.15) The citizenry must control the levying of taxes. (71:8.6) Excessive taxation under national governments obstructs individual freedoms but will diminish under a world government. (134:6.10) The ideal state must support state power without handicapping industry, regulating social conduct to prevent unfairness and maintain peace in industry. (71:5.2) historically in continental nation of nearby inhabited planet inheritance tax in the future

name: taxpayers
description: The improvident expect to be fed by thrifty taxpayers, perpetuating a form of modern slavery. (69:9.5) Heavy taxpayers in this continental nation receive extra votes based on the amount of taxes paid, with a maximum of five additional votes permitted. (72:9.5)

name: teachers
description: The ascendant plan of mortal progression involves ascending by teaching those behind us in the scale of progression, continually giving out new truth and experience as acquired. (30:3.9) (30:4.23) (100:2.1) Beware of false teachers who will arise and lead many astray, for they will deceive even in times of great tribulation. (176:1.4) Teachers of the kingdom are to respect the personality of man, avoid coercion, and appeal directly to the divine spirit within each individual. (159:3) Jesus maintained his integrity by remaining a learner, unlike the proud professors at the university in Alexandria. (130:3.7) Teachers must be free beings, real leaders, in order to ensure that education prioritizes philosophy and the search for wisdom. (71:7.4) Perfectors of Wisdom are the ultimate source of wisdom for all teachers, guiding the application of universe knowledge and providing fountains of discretion and wellsprings of discrimination in all institutions of learning. (19:2.3)

name: teachers of rest
description: Teachers of rest counsel seraphim on energy intake for efficient energy utilization and balance maintenance among active cherubim and passive sanobim. (44:5.10) Celestial artisans and energy manipulators are the teachers of rest, guiding us to replenish our spiritual energies through divine rest. (44:5.8)

name: teaching counselors
description: Assistant teachers are helpers and associates of teaching counselors, connected with educational enterprises and the sevenfold training scheme in the local universe, with a marvelous corps on Urantia fostering truth and righteousness. (39:2.7) Mota enhances philosophy like two eyes bring depth to sight, thanks to the guidance of many morontia counselor transition seraphim teaching counselors. (48:6.28) Michael's 4th bestowal was as a teaching counselor of the supreme seraphim in Nebadon. (39:1.15) The Teaching Counselors, secretaries to all teachers, from Melchizedeks to mortals, are invaluable assistants to the spiritual teaching corps of the local universe. (39:1.13-14)

name: tears
description: The brothel keeper anointed Jesus’ feet with her tears of gratitude. (147:5.3) God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes, for the former things have passed away. (27:1.5)

name: Technical Advisers
description: Technical Advisers are devoted to interpreting laws for all beings and assisting in understanding how the Supremacy of Deity will react in various situations, including elucidating the technique of the Ultimate. (25:4.19) The Melchizedeks counsel Life Carriers and Universal Censors in the techniques and usages of spirit-world transactions. (25:4.18) The Technical Advisers play a crucial role in guiding various celestial beings and entities on the proper usages and techniques of spiritual transactions, but they do not interact directly with mortals. (25:4.18) Technical Advisers have never gone astray in their divine interpretation and expounding of law and experience, extending their service to the portals of Paradise without limit. (25:4.20) Technical Advisers are in Uversa personality register. (30:2.77) Living law libraries teach the applied laws of the universe to those journeying toward their Paradise goal. (25:4.12,15-16) (37:8.7) Technical Advisers maintain local rendezvous on mansion worlds and Jerusem circles. (46:5.21) (47:0.4) The number of Technical Advisers is slightly in excess of sixty-one trillion on Uversa in Orvonton, with about half a billion in Nebadon. (25:4.10) (37:8.7) Once enrolled, Technical Advisers may not transfer out, only the Ancients of Days have the authority to do so. (25:4.13) The original group of Technical Advisers was chosen from one million of the most orderly minds in the spirit world by the Infinite Spirit. (25:4.1) The Technical Advisers counsel achievement and prevent endless trouble and delays by instructing beings on the laws of the universe and the divine method of perfection. (25:4.15,17) Technical Advisers are recruited from supernaphim, seconaphim, tertiaphim, omniaphim, seraphim, mortal ascenders, and midwayers from various personality orders. (25:4.2) (27:0.1) residence in Jerusem circles

name: technicians
description: The 5th order transition minister seraphim educate and guide mortals on the seven mansion worlds in Satania. (39:6.1) Technicians serve as liaisons between survivors and Morontia Power Supervisors and Master Physical Controllers, helping ascenders adjust to the morontia spheres. (48:6.29) Technicians, space traversers, help morontia progressors adjust to new spheres of existence. (48:6.29)

name: teeth
description: Early mammals of the Cenozoic developed two sets of teeth, had multiple mammary glands, were covered in hair, and had large brains relative to body size, but no modern forms were present among them. (61:1.10) Human teeth are slowly evolving toward 28 as part of an ongoing adaptative process of evolution. (65:6.5) Magical charms concocted from human flesh, tiger claws, crocodile teeth, poison plant seeds, snake venom, human hair, bones of the dead, footprints dust, and bodily secretions were believed to hold great magical power, especially in love spells by the ancients. (88:5.2)

name: Teherma the Persian
description: Teherma the Persian, not a fire worshipper, found enlightenment in Jesus' teachings at Capernaum. (141:6.1-3)

name: telepathy
description: Adam and Eve communicated through thought oscillations transmitted up to 50 miles, but lost the ability upon succumbing to evil. (74:6.6)

name: telescopes
description: Spectral lines show red shift indicating the apparent recession of far-distant systems at incredible speeds. (12:4.14) New telescopes will reveal 375 million galaxies in outer space, unveiling the vast and expanding universe beyond our current knowledge. (12:2.2-3)

name: temperament
description: Inherent temperament plays a significant role in shaping one's religious philosophy and experience. (100:1.6) (101:7.1) Despite variations in technique, man's inherent disposition towards wealth accumulation and generosity remains unchanged throughout history. (69:5.13)

name: temperance
description: The fruits of the Spirit - love, joy, peace, long-suffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, and temperance - illuminate the path of spirit-led mortals towards eternal life and the kingdom of God. (34:6.13) Jesus preached temperance and taught consistency through proportionate adjustment of life problems, warning against extremes in virtue that may lead to vice. (149:4.3)

name: temperature
description: At the center of the earth, temperatures exceed those of the surface of the sun due to immense pressure and heat. (58:5.1) Gravity prevents the existence of absolute zero in the interstellar space, filled with energy currents and ultimatonic activities. (42:4.6) In the earth's atmosphere, temperature varies from 70 degrees below zero F in the stratosphere to 1200° F at higher altitudes. (58:2.6) The internal temperatures of suns, including your own, are much higher than commonly believed due to the intense X-ray bombardment, reaching an unbelievable 35,000,000 degrees in the central regions. (41:7) (41:9.1) Temperature, second only to gravity in energy and matter evolution, is the openly recognized transmutation of space potency into primordial force. (42:2.7)

name: Temple of New Life
description: The Temple of New Life extends seven radial wings, housing resurrection halls for the assembly of the seven races of time, each with one hundred thousand personal chambers leading to awakening chambers for up to one million individuals, surrounded by personality assembly chambers for blended races, where real and conscious reassembly of personality occurs on mansonia number one. (47:3.5)

name: temple
description: All worlds of mortal ascent have exquisite temples of worship built by experienced architects of the spirit. (44:3.5) Upon the rock of spiritual reality, Jesus declared he would build the living temple of spiritual fellowship in the eternal realities of his Father's kingdom. (157:4.5,7) Take men into the temple before showing them its beauties, for first they must be reborn of the spirit to truly understand the profound teachings within. (141:6.4) The value of beauty leading up to worship was emphasized by Jesus in teaching his apostles about the importance of simplicity and natural surroundings in connecting with God. (167:6.5-6) In light and life, temples and open-air arenas of worship are creations of beauty and grandeur, devoted to the training of mind and expansion of soul. (55:5.5) temple of God temples in ancient times temples on Urantia in a nearby planet celestial temples

name: temptation
description: One cannot overcome temptation through mere human will; true victory lies in developing a love for higher ideals and conduct through spiritual transformation. (156:5.5) Transform temptation by redirecting energies into higher channels and enter the spirit kingdom to enjoy comparative deliverance from the slave-bondage of the flesh, following the new way of mortal living revealed by Jesus. (34:7.6) (156:5.4) Adam's one hundred years on earth concluded with Serapatatia, influenced by temptation and flattery, convincing Eve to embark on a secret, ill-fated project. (75:3) Jesus’ great temptations

name: ten-cent piece
description: The temple courts were profaned by the extensive system of banking and commercial exchange, where money-changers charged high commissions for the exchange of a coin about the size of a ten-cent piece. (173:1.3)

name: Ten Commandments
description: The two versions of the Ten Commandments reflect the evolving understanding of divine law and the changing concepts of divinity. (142:3.10-22) Jesus wrote the Ten Commandments in Greek on two boards, which were later destroyed by him. (126:4.9) (137:2.9) The taboos of Yahweh, promulgated by Moses, replaced thousands of pre-existent prohibitions and promised rewards for obedience. (89:1.4) (96:4.4) God's Ten Commandments, recorded by Ganid, bring great peace to those who love his law. (131:2.12)

name: ten virgins
description: The parable of the ten virgins, from the Master's discourse on the destruction of Jerusalem and his second coming, recorded by Selta, caused great confusion among the apostles, except for John. (176:2.8)

name: tenderness
description: The evidence that man is indwelt by God is shown through manifestations of divine mercy and tenderness towards the weak and needy, illustrating the unique blending of human and Creator. (26:11.5) It is a great error to teach boys that it is unmanly to show tenderness; true courage and happiness come from being sensitive and responsive to human needs. (140:5.16) Indiscriminate kindness may breed social evils, Jesus preached wisdom, tolerance, and forgiveness. (140:8.13) The mature human looks upon all others with tenderness and tolerance, embracing the journey towards emotional and intellectual maturity. (160:1.6)

name: Tenskwatawa
description: Tenskwatawa prophesied the 1808 eclipse, denouncing shamanism and white man's vices to challenge shamans against scientific progress. (90:2.9)

name: tentmaker
description: Jesus worked as a tentmaker in Antioch, unknowingly preparing the way for the coming of Peter, Paul, and other Christian teachers in Rome. (132:0.10) (134:7.3) Paul was a devotee of the cult of renunciation and humiliation, influencing Christianity with teachings that looked down upon women and could lead to the end of the human race if universally obeyed. (89:3.6)

name: Terah
description: The children of Terah, father of Abraham, fulfilled the expectations of the Melchizedek receivers with their intelligence, initiative, sagacity, and sincerity. (93:5.3-5)

name: terminal rest of time
description: Before achieving residential status on Paradise, ascendant souls undergo a terminal rest of time following their divine rest and final clearance of the Havona circuits. (26:7.4)

name: terrestrial escape
description: The ministry of Thought Adjusters, guardian angels, and messenger hosts of the Infinite Spirit is essential to the liberation of all mortals from the bonds of flesh, enabling terrestrial escape to the morontia worlds of spiritual evolution and mind progress. (49:6) (55:2)

name: tertiaphim
description: When a Michael Son is ready to embark on the adventure of universe organization, 1000 tertiaphim accompany him and retire with the appearance of native angels. (28:1.2-3) Michael, accompanied by a lone tertiaphim, departed his headquarters sphere and a new Lanonandek Son was appointed as acting System Sovereign. (119:2.4) The tertiaphim, as children of the Infinite Spirit, serve in liaison between Creator Sons and the Ancients of Days. (26:1.13) (28:1.1) Tertiaphim are dedicated to facilitating liaisons between Creator Sons and Ancients of Days. (26:1.13) The Tertiaphim may become Technical Advisers. (25:4.5) The tertiaphim retire from active service in the local universe when native angels appear. (28:1.3) Tertiaphim serve as Most High Assistants in the ministry of Paradise-origin beings. (37:4.2) Tertiaphim's appear registered in personality registers. (30:1.83) (30:2.85)

name: Tertiary Circuit Supervisors
description: Circuit supervisors direct spirit messages and personalities with efficiency, utilizing all forces in the universe, but they cannot annul material currents. (24:1.12) Circuit supervisors, employing high spirit personalities of circuit control, direct all spirit messages and personalities across the universe. (24:1.12) Tertiary Circuit Supervisors function on the headquarters world of every local universe, created in groups of seven hundred and are assigned by the Ancients of Days, serving eternally in their original assignment. (24:1.6,10-11) Satania was quarantined by the action of the Tertiary Circuit Supervisors. (53:7.3)

name: testimonies
description: A Divine Counselor said: “I attest, as a Divine Counselor, to the eternal truth of the Father's love and spirit, revealed through the glorious personality of the Eternal Son.” (1:3.8) (1:7.9) (6:8.8) The unbiased testimonies secured by reflective seconaphim provide crucial insight for the angelic hosts at council sessions. (28:4.11)

name: tests
description: Jesus passed the great test of refusing to use power selfishly, choosing to establish the kingdom of heaven in the hearts of mankind through natural, ordinary, difficult, and trying methods. (136:8.6) Jesus increasingly measured society and religion by the test of what it does for the human soul, bringing God to man and man to God. (126:2.5)

name: Thaddeus
description: James Alpheus, also called Thaddeus, embraced his role as one of the “least of all the apostles” with cheerful humility. (139:10.5)

name: Thamna
description: Jesus passed through Thamna on his journey preaching the good news of the kingdom. (143:0.1)

name: Thapsacus
description: Jesus passed through Thapsacus on his journey through Mesopotamia. (130:0.3)

name: Thebes
description: Tutankhamen returned capital to Thebes, where the priests gained power and eventually seized the crown. (95:5.10)

name: theft
description: Among primitives, theft only occurred outside the group due to the belief that personal property held the essence of the owner's identity. (69:9.9) The commandments against theft are an integral part of loving and obeying God. (66:7.13) (93:4.11) (94:8.11) (131:2.12) (163:2.4) Stealing evolved as the great crime amid the advancement of civilization and the recognition of private property rights, intertwining with the historical view of women as property and the enforcement of marital fidelity through the evolving mores. (82:4.3-4)

name: theology
description: God-consciousness is a universal reality that remains consistent, but the philosophical concept and theological definitions of God must evolve with each era as human knowledge advances. (5:5.12) Theology does not produce religion; religion develops values first, followed by interpretative beliefs. (103:1.4) The mission of theology is to define God and justify claims of religion, facilitating personal spiritual experience and living faith. (90:5.7) (103:9.6) The mission of theology is to facilitate self-consciousness of spiritual experience and validate experiential claims through living faith. (103:9.6) In theology, facts must not displace truth in the central teachings of the kingdom of God as derived from the life and teachings of Jesus of Nazareth. (143:6.3) The fallacy that any creed is uniquely The Truth impedes the progress and evolution of religion, as real growth requires the humility to learn from other faiths. (92:3.4) (92:7.3) Jesus's personal, living, and purely spiritual faith, untouched by theology's fixes and dogmas, was unshakable in the face of all trials. (196:0.5) Nalda dodged the issue of salvation with a discussion of theology and philosophy, despite her true thirst for living water. (143:5.5-7) The philosophy of religion, theology, interprets the values and personal experiences that validate religion, even if beliefs may contain elements of truth and error. (103:1.5) The paradox of human comprehension in theology, the psychology of religion, stems from the struggle to reconcile the concept of divine immanence with God's transcendence in religious experience. (5:5.6) Despite erroneous theology, religion can still be genuine and everlasting. (103:9.1-2) Theology is the study of one's religion, approached from inside, never becoming a science but intertwined with psychology and philosophy. (103:6.1-2) theologians

name: theories
description: The highest religious doctrines are dead and powerless unless transformed through divine spirit and power, as declared by your teacher of old. (34:6.6) Experience always has advantage over theory. (102:6.7) Facts always support spiritual faith, while theories may challenge it. (195:7.1) You cannot confide in a postulate, just as you cannot pray to a chemical formula or worship a hypothesis. (102:7.3) Imprisoning divine truth in human formulations leads to post-mortem salvage of dead theories, while living truth thrives in spirit-discerned realities. (180:5.2)

name: thieves
description: A good Samaritan helped a man who was robbed and beaten. (164:1.3) Thieves killed the rich man with full barns, ending his earthly wealth and leaving him without treasures in heaven. (165:4.3)

name: thieves crucified with Jesus
description: The two thieves, associates of Barabbas, were scourged before crucifixion and crucified with Jesus in place of their leader. (187:0.1-2) The soldiers took the bodies of the thieves to Gehenna after placing Jesus' body in the tomb. (188:1.5) Thieves crucified with Jesus cast reproach upon him, but one thief sought forgiveness and received salvation in Paradise. (187:3.3) (187:4.1) The two brigands, dispatched by Roman soldiers, caused a delay in taking Jesus down from the cross before his burial and resurrection. (188:0.1) “Lord, remember me when you come into your kingdom,” the repentant thief said, earning Jesus' approval and the promise of future salvation in Paradise. (187:4.1) One of the thieves crucified with Jesus had often heard him speak. (186:4.4-5) The penitent brigand, inspired by Jesus on the cross, renounced his prior belief in robbery as a patriotic protest. (187:4.5)

name: third day
description: When the bestowal Sons, whether Creator or Magisterial, pass through death, they reappear on the third day, not always facing the tragic fate of Jesus of Nazareth on Urantia. (20:6.6) A newborn Graduate Guide appears on the third day after a servital's career is closed. (24:7.6) The prodigious number of Havona servitals, created constantly, appear in groups of one thousand on the third moment, with every fourth being more physical in nature. (25:1.3) Jesus would rise on the third day as he had forewarned his disciples. (171:4.2,8) (186:3.4) (188:1.8) (188:2.1,2) (189:5.1) (190:1.3,5) (190:5.3-4) (191:0.2) The apostles could not grasp the warnings of the impending setback, interpreting Jesus' mention of “rising on the third day” as a sign of imminent triumph in the kingdom. (171:4.8) The Jews believed that the soul would not linger past the third day, as decay was well advanced by the fourth day. (167:4.3)

name: Third Source personalities
description: The Infinite Spirit bestows a vast array of Third Source personalities, beings personal to Him but not in the Father's personality circuit, upon various groups in the grand universe. (9:8.6-12) Third Source personalities are contactable by beings in Father's personality circuit. (9:8.9) Spirit-fused mortals are not Third Source personalities but are included in the Father’s personality circuit, having fused with individualizations of the premind spirit of the Third Source and Center. (40:9.1) Supreme Power Centers are beings of high will freedom and action, endowed with Third-Source personality and exquisite intelligence. (29:2.9)

name: thirst
description: Jesus expressed his thirst while on the cross, prompting the captain of the guard to offer him wine on a javelin. (187:3.5) In the Divine Presence, there is no thirst, only the eternal satisfaction of spiritual enlightenment and moral strength. (34:6.8) (48:6.11) Believe in Jesus, never thirst spiritually, and receive eternal life. (153:2.7-9)

name: Thomas Didymus
description: personal life attitude after Jesus

name: Thor (Andite)
description: Thor, victorious commander in the final battle of Somme, led the white invaders to defeat the Cro-Magnoids in a five-hundred year struggle. (80:5.4)

name: Thor (god)
description: The master of lightning, Thor, emerged victorious in the decisive struggles between the white man and the blue man in the valley of the Somme. (80:5.4) (85:6.4)

name: thorns
description: A crown of thorns placed on Jesus' head symbolized his suffering, ignored by those consumed by hatred and prejudice. (185:6.2,4) Some seed fell among thorns, choked and unfruitful due to worldly cares and riches. (151:1.2) (151:2.2)

name: Those High in Authority
description: The cream of governing ability, Those High in Authority, are Adjuster-fused mortals with superior administrative ability. (22:3.1) Those High in Authority, glorified ascendant mortals, are the everywhere-present and always-efficient executives of the Ancients of Days in the superuniverse government. (15:10.3-12) (22:3.3) Those High in Authority, as Trinitized Sons of Attainment, were embraced by Trinity after ascending through the regime, passing through Havona, and being mustered into the Corps of the Finality. (15:10.12) High Son Assistants serve as clerks for Those High in Authority, acting as personal aids to the high sons of the governments of the Ancients of Days. (22:10.1) Those High in Authority in Uversa personality register. (30:2.56) The Perfectors of Wisdom, Those High in Authority, and Divine Counselors represent the union of divine insight and experiential knowledge, achieving the maximum of administrative wisdom in the superuniverse. (19:2.4) (19:3.6-7) There are seventy billion skillful administrators of Those High in Authority commissioned in Orvonton, with seventy thousand being trinitized at each Trinity liaison. (22:3.2) Paper 25 was sponsored by One High in Authority from Uversa. (25:8.12) Those High in Authority portray the attitude of the higher government in superuniverse courts, fostering the execution of justice and rectification of misadaptations in evolutionary universes. (15:12.1) (22:3.4) The selection of candidates for Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number, is inherently and automatically determined by personal experience and spiritual values, not by arbitrary means. (22:4.4) The routine work of major sector affairs is carried out by Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants, with Those High in Authority overseeing service on major sectors. (18:4.4) Those High in Authority advance individuals with augmented trusts according to their character development, while ensuring that they are not overloaded to prevent disaster and disappointment. (28:6.15) Tertiary seconaphim are assigned to Those High in Authority among the ascendant trinitized associates of the superuniverse rulers. (28:3.1) (28:6.1) Those High in Authority, as Trinitized Sons of Attainment, are Adjuster-fused mortal beings of extraordinary executive genius who represent the attitude of the higher government in the superuniverse. (15:10.12) (15:12.1) (22:3.1) (28:6.1)

name: Those without Name and Number
description: The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Those without Name and Number, were embraced by the Trinity and assigned to the supernal service of the Ancients of Days, enlarging the executive branch of the superuniverse government. (15:10.12) Those without Name and Number have a transcendent concept of the Father's eternal purpose. (22:4.1) High Son Assistants serve as clerks for the superior group of the retrinitized trinitized sons of glorified ascendant beings in the Mortal Corps of the Finality and their eternal associates. (22:10.1) Those without Name and Number, members of highest mobile advisory body, assist in achieving the maximum of administrative wisdom by combining divine insight and personal experience from evolutionary progression. (19:2.4) (19:3.6-7) There are over 700 million Sons without Name and Number embraced by the Paradise Trinity in groups of seven thousand. (22:4.2) The high commission of Orvonton administrators sponsored, formulated, and put into English Paper 31 depicting the nature of Deity, the reality of Paradise, the organization and working of the central and superuniverses, the personalities of the grand universe, and the high destiny of evolutionary mortals. (31:10.22) Selection for the trinitization classes is inherently automatic, with personal experience and spiritual values determining the diverse characters of Those without Name and Number. (22:4.4) Those without Name and Number serve superuniverse government as Trinitized Sons of Attainment. (15:10.3-12) The routine work of major sector affairs is primarily handled by Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants, while Those without Name and Number play a significant role in the governance of the superuniverse. (18:4.4) Those without Name and Number, as the superior spiritual minds of survival races, are the supreme jurors of Orvonton, entrusted to render opinions and sit in judgment with a deep understanding of the ascendant career, ensuring justice is served in the highest realms of adjudication. (22:4.3) Those without Name and Number are the supreme jurors in superuniverse courts. (15:12.1) (22:4.3) The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including Mighty Messengers and Those High in Authority, embrace Those without Name and Number, who worship beyond comprehension and are denominated “Those above Name and Number.” (15:10.12) (22:4.1) (28:6.1) Those without Name and Number in Uversa are registered in personality records. (30:2.57)

name: Thoth
description: Thoth's spittle healed Set's eye, illustrating the widespread belief in spittle as a healing agent originating in Egypt. (95:2.6)

name: thought
description: Abrupt presentations of subconscious emanations in the mind are sometimes influenced by the Adjuster, while more often arising from natural psychic functions inherent in the evolving animal mind. (110:4.3) As a man thinks in his heart, so is he, trusting in Yahweh with all his heart, acknowledging him in all ways, and receiving divine guidance. (131:2.8) (131:5.2) Celestial beings excel in ideograph techniques to infinitely enhance thought preservation beyond concept picturization. (44:4.6) Attaining cosmologic levels of thought involves a hunger for harmony and beauty, a love of the beautiful, and a sensitivity to ethics that leads to the pursuit of divine reality values. (56:10.5-8) The Conjoint Actor unerringly draws all divine thoughts into the absolute circuit of universal mind. (9:6.2) Dreams illustrate the struggle between unexpressed desires and divine messages during unconscious sleep. (110:5.4) Few mortals are real thinkers, spiritually developing and disciplining their minds to favorably connect with their divine Adjusters. (110:7.6) The word God symbolizes the approach to supreme reality and recognition of divine value, with human likes and dislikes not determining good and evil. (100:3.2) The spread of civilization hinges on the effectiveness of language as the greatest thinking tool, requiring further development to express evolving thought. (81:6.16-17) Let the unrighteous man forsake his thoughts and return to the Lord, for he will abundantly pardon. (97:7.7) Feeling, not thought, was the guiding force in evolutionary development, blurring the lines between fear, avoidance, respect, and worship in the primitive mind. (85:7.2) Recognize and disregard the interference of preconceived opinions, settled ideas, and long-standing prejudices, in order to allow for the prompt recognition of every thought for what it truly is. (109:5.3) Religion must navigate the paradox of utilizing thought while recognizing the limitations of intellectual overdevelopment in attaining higher spiritual attainment. (102:3.1) Melody of thought—the thinking of spiritual thoughts can be so perfected as to burst forth in the melodies of Havona. (44:1.8) Thought, not feelings, lead us Godward as the divine spirit makes contact with mortal man in the realm of the highest and most spiritualized thinking. (101:1.3) God's thoughts are higher than man's, but deliverance from guilt comes from realizing one's highest ideals may not align with God's will. (103:4.3) (131:2.3) Life on the divine worlds of the central universe transcends human concepts, including the technique of Havona thought unlike ours on Urantia. (14:5.2)

name: Thought Adjuster of Jesus
aliases: the Personalized Adjuster of his indwelling
description: The Personalized Adjuster of Jesus, chief of his kind in Nebadon, guided him faithfully and wisely through his human life, leading him to the Father's perfect will. (109:6.4-6) (136:2.3,5) (168:2.6) The Thought Adjuster of Jesus, who previously served with Machiventa Melchizedek, guided him step by step during his human life and aided in the choosing of the path of the Father's perfect will. (109:6.4) (123:2.1) (136:2.2) The Thought Adjuster of Jesus collaborated in creation of Part IV, utilizing existing records from the life of Jesus on Urantia, including the Gospels of Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. (121:8.1) (168:1.2) The Thought Adjuster arrived with Jesus on February 11, 2 B.C., marking his first personal moral decision. (123:2.1) (196:1.7) The advice of Gabriel was to let Jesus, as a Creator Son, have the power to resurrect himself. (189:0.1-3) At healing at sundown, Jesus passed the responsibility of this decision to his Father, resulting in a miraculous wave of healing for 683 individuals. (145:3.10-11) at Jesus’ baptism during Jesus life moments Thought Adjuster spoke

name: Thought Adjusters
description: Adjusters, serving all local universes, are organized as an independent unit administered directly from Divinington, functioning interchangeably throughout the grand universe. (108:3.1) Father fragments are fully cognizant of the universal reflectivity system and can avail themselves of its content. (17:3.9) The better adapted secondary midwayers are able to attain contact with Thought Adjusters of certain mortals, mobilizing spiritual civilization through reserve corps. (114:7.9) The circuits of Thought Adjusters allow for direct communication with the Universal Father. (5:3.2) (15:9.6) The divine presence is most fully discovered in communion with the indwelling Mystery Monitor, the Paradise Thought Adjuster. (5:2.3-4) During transit sleeps between mansion worlds, the Thought Adjuster remains with you, ensuring your personality stays intact throughout your journey of spiritual advancement. (47:4.4) The greatest days in Thought Adjusters' individual careers occur when the human subject breaks through into the third psychic circle, attains the first psychic circle, and ultimately fuses eternally. (110:6.22) Human subjects are often known by the numbers of their Adjusters until after fusion, when they receive real universe names bestowed by destiny guardians. (108:3.3) If the Adjusters were withdrawn, Urantia would regress to barbarism, as they play a crucial role in transforming and humanizing its inhabitants. (109:4.4) Master Spirit Number One, the direct representation of the Paradise Father, is the close associate of the chief of Mystery Monitors and speaks for the Universal Father in all associations of the Seven Master Spirits. (16:3.2) The mystery of divine indwelling is the profoundest of universe mysteries, revealing the eternal perfection of God. (1:4.1,3) (124:6.17) Adjusters and seraphim work in perfect harmony, though they do not directly communicate. (113:4.5) (113:5.1) Even if subjects fail to survive, Thought Adjusters are not examined, as their flawless technique is beyond criticism. (108:5.10) Though we have limited knowledge of Adjusters, it is clear they are organized independently and administered directly from Divinington, serving throughout the superuniverses with a serial organization that extends across races, dispensations, and worlds. (108:3) Thought Adjusters, being of the essence of the original Deity, proceed directly from the Universal Father and are fragmentized entities constituting the factual presence of the infinite God, undiluted and unmixed divinity. (107:1) Surviving individuals rejoin resurrected subjects after their identity transition is realized. (112:4.13) Upon death, your Thought Adjuster will proceed to the Father, while angels are not involved in your transition to a morontia form. (39:2.13) When mortals fail to achieve eternal survival, their Adjusters serve on Divinington until resurrection, never neglecting duty or service. (40:4.1) (40:9.2) (49:6.5) (112:4) (112:4.4-11) Solitary Messengers and Inspired Trinity Spirits can detect presence of divine Thought Adjusters through spiritual reactive phenomena. (23:1.9) (107:4.4) (108:3.9) To secure planetary release and begin the Paradise career, spiritual beings must humanize to receive Thought Adjusters after the morontia experience. (55:4.25,31) Thought Adjuster fusion imparts eternal actualities to personality which were previously only potential, culminating in the mystery of making God and man one, leading to an eternal embrace and eventual ascension to higher realms. About mortal fusion with Thought Adjusters see link on fusion (112:7) | fusion:Adjuster fusion arrival historically also known as... mission and function of Thought Adjusters mortal co-operation with Adjusters mortal communication with Adjusters nature of Adjusters types of Adjusters types of Adjusters, other classifications

name: Thought Changers
description: Thought Changers guide individuals until age 20, then become Thought Adjusters. (107:0.7)

name: Thought Controllers
description: Thought Controllers are Adjusters called when subjects are older than forty. (107:0.7)

name: thought preservers
description: Thought preservers, celestial artisans, also preserve the noble ideas of great Urantia minds in the language of Uversa. (44:4.2-4)

name: thought recorders
description: Celestial artisans devoted to preserving and reproducing superior thoughts of the realms function in seven groups, including thought preservers, concept recorders, and ideograph recorders. (44:4) types

name: thoughtless pagan
description: Jesus’ comment to Ganid regarding the thoughtless pagan emphasized the importance of being receptive to the teachings of God in order to truly understand and seek salvation. (132:7.1,2)

name: Thrace
description: Jews in Thrace encompassed Matthew's death. (139:7.10) Thracians believed in immortality, unlike many other ancient tribes whose religious practices have evolved only minimally over time. (92:6.1)

name: three-brained mortals
description: Three-brained mortals exhibit a higher level of spiritual, ethical, and worshipful attributes compared to two-brained and one-brained mortals. (49:5.13-18)

name: threefold fluctuation
description: The threefold fluctuation of light from Deity abode indicates complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents. (27:7.7)

name: threshold
description: The threefold fluctuation of light from the Deity abode signifies complete satisfaction of the Gods by the worship of Paradise residents, engulfing all of Paradise in dominating tide of spiritual expression. (27:7.7)

name: throwing stick
description: The Andonites crafted the throwing stick for successful hunting expeditions. (63:5.6)

name: thumbs
description: One million years ago, Mesopotamian dawn mammals emerged with primitive opposable thumbs, descendants of the North American lemur type of placental mammal. (62:2.1) The mid-mammals had perfectly opposable thumbs, just about as well adapted for diversified work as the present human thumb, and walked upright with feet almost as well suited for walking as the later human races. (62:3.2)

name: thunder
description: James and John, the sons of thunder, were transformed by Jesus into the apostles of love. (139:3.1,5) (139:4.6) (181:2.4) Thunder, the voice of an angry god, overawed early man and was feared and worshiped by primitive tribes and nature cults. (85:4.3)

name: thunderstones
description: Bretons protect against lightning with ancient thunderstones, a tradition passed down from the original Andonite inhabitants of western Europe. (80:9.14)

name: Thursday
description: Thursday, a once lucky day for weddings, was believed to bring the most favorable outcomes when celebrating under the full moon, with the custom of throwing grain to ensure fecundity. (83:4.5)

name: Ti Tao
description: Fifteen thousand years ago, Andites entered China via the pass of Ti Tao, eventually spreading throughout the upper Yellow River valley and progressing eastward to Honan. (79:7.1)

name: Tiberias
description: Jesus succeeded Sepphoris, spending a week in Tiberias before moving on to other cities around the Sea of Galilee. (129:1.1-2) (135:12.1) At Tiberias, conferences of Herod and Sanhedrin were held to plot the arrest and trial of Jesus, despite Herod's initial refusal. (154:0.1) (154:3) James of Safed from Tiberias sought Jesus for his demon-possessed epileptic son. (158:4.2) Jesus told apostles to avoid Sepphoris and Tiberias in their future work. (137:5.1) Thomas, the leading citizen of Tarichea, was the son of excellent parents who lived at Tiberias. (139:8.2)

name: Tiberius
description: Jesus was baptized in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, who was coemperor with Augustus beginning in A.D. 26. (136:2.8) Tiberius allowed Pontius Pilate to serve as procurator of Judea for a decade due to his competence in governing minor provinces, despite his moral cowardice and failure to understand the significance of Judaism. (185:1.1) Gonod carried greetings from the princes of India to Tiberius, who admired Jesus' kingly bearing and gracious manner. (132:0.1) Tiberius admired Jesus' bearing and wished for his own kingly and gracious manner. (132:0.1) Tiberius died while Pilate was on his way to Rome, never recovering from the regret of sacrificing Jesus and eventually being deposed. (185:1.6)

name: Tibet
description: Andonites in Tibet lived in crude stone huts and hillside grottoes, surrounded by forests and streams, abounding in game. (64:3.2) Andite migration to central Asia greatly influenced the ancestors of the Turanians and contributed valuable qualities to the Chinese stocks, with some of the early migrations extending into Tibet. (78:5.6) (79:1.1-2,8) Asoka established Buddhism in Tibet, spreading the religion throughout eastern Asia and making it the dominant faith in half the world. (94:9.1) Early Chinese settlements near Tibet were well established in control of eastern Asia. (78:1.7) Tibetans embrace elements of all world religions except the gospel of Jesus: sonship with God, brotherhood with man, and eternal citizenship in the universe. (94:10.3) Tibetans made little contact with the Chinese until recent times, despite the yellow race entering China over 300,000 years ago. (79:5.3) Migration from Tibet to the Yangtze valley added Andite blood to the river settlements, although not as extensively as in the north. (79:6.5) In Tibet, the shamanic priests and medicine men became wealthy by accumulating fees as offerings to spirits, often possessing most of the tribe's wealth, with half the male population belonging to this nonproducing class. (90:2.11) Religion in Tibet combines Melchizedek teachings with Buddhism, Hinduism, Taoism, and Christianity, resulting in an elaborate and diverse ceremonial practice. (94:10)

name: tidal friction
description: Tidal friction gradually stops planets' revolutions, leading to stable orbits and potential moon fragmentation. (57:6.2-3)

name: tidal wave
description: A tidal wave swept over Dalamatia, causing the planetary headquarters to sink beneath the sea, leading to the near-obliteration of its noble culture. (67:5.4)

name: tigers
description: If unarmed man met a hungry tiger, society's prime gift to man is security, as nature confers no rights on man. (70:9.1)

name: Tiglath
description: Tiglath was the lad that helped Jesus on Mount Hermon with supplies, and in return Jesus asked for guardian seraphim to be with him, and gave him a donkey as a present. (134:8.1,2,10)

name: Tigris River
description: The Tigris and Euphrates rivers were the cradle of Occidental and Indian civilizations, giving life to the violet race and spreading their culture to surrounding lands. (78:1.3) The second garden adjoined the Tigris River, where Adam and his followers established a new center of culture and religion. (76:0.1) (76:1.1-3) (76:3.8) Sumerians lived at the mouth of the Tigris River, persisting for thousands of years to furnish the Nodite ancestry of the Sumerian peoples. (77:4.6) (78.8)

name: time
description: Time is eternity, the absolute limit. (12:5.2) Time alone ripens fruit on tree, everything must await the coming of its time. (130:5.3) Animals lack self-consciousness of time, unlike man who progresses inward to perceive time as a whole cycle, moving beyond the linear sequence of events towards circular simultaneity. (130:7.5) (133:7.8) Faith in Jesus brings salvation from time, granting eternal life of unending progression in God-recognition and God-service. (101:6.14) Consulting the Imports of Time enables the forecasting of future events with the same reliability as other indicators used by the authorities of the universes of time. (28:6.11) Lucifer's futile attempt to short-circuit time threatened the freewill choice of all beings in Satania and stood as a monumental threat to steal personal liberty on an unprecedented scale. (54:2.3) (118:8.7,9) days and years function of time nature of time wisely using time independence of time

name: timidity
description: Andrew occasionally manifested dangerous timidity, but always maintained sincere justice and fairness in his leadership role among the messengers of the heavenly kingdom. (181:2.16) The God-knowing individual triumphs over obstacles with living faith, while many who are certain about God fear to assert their beliefs in the face of shallow objections. (102:7.6)

name: Timothy
description: Paul, joined by Silas and Timothy, preached in Corinth for eighteen months and encountered students of the “Jewish tutor of the son of an Indian merchant.” (133:3.3)

name: tin
description: The Adamsonites mixed copper with tin to create bronze, revolutionizing metalworking. (81:3.5)

name: tithing
description: sethites and Abraham Jesus' times light and life

name: Titus (associate of Paul)
description: Fortune, once a youth, became the leader of the Christians in Crete and closely assisted Titus in uplifting believers. (130:6.5) Paul sent Titus to reorganize the churches in Crete despite the Cretans' questionable reputation. (130:5.1)

name: Titus (Emperor)
description: Titus, who destroyed Jerusalem in A.D. 70, brought about a terrible harvest of death and destruction upon the Jewish people. (121:8.7) (187:1.5)

name: Titus of Capernaum
description: Titus of Capernaum, a nobleman with a critically ill son, believed in Jesus and witnessed a miraculous healing. (146:5.1-3) Titus of Capernaum was among the nobleman's son trained for gospel service in Peter's school before being ordained by Jesus. (148:1.4)

name: Toda tribe
description: The Toda tribe of India regressed to primitive levels by praying to no one in particular, neglecting social, moral, and spiritual values in their rituals. (91:0.4) The semimagical recitations of the Toda tribe have evolved into a more intelligent and ethical form of prayer over time. (91:3.2)

name: Todan
description: Todan first heard the gospel at Rimmon and later brought the message to Mesopotamia and beyond. (146:1.4)

name: tokens
description: Civilized races cursed with belief in signs and tokens seek to avoid ill luck through superstitious remnants of an ancient ghost cult. (87:5.14)

name: tolerance
description: Understanding others leads to tolerance, which matures into love. (100:4.4) Differences in status are essential to the development of human tolerance and altruism. (64:6.34) Education evolves conciliators into interpretive teachers, increasing respect for others' opinions and avoiding clashes of mind. (25:3.12) Tolerance is essential for success in persuading and prevailing with others. (160:1.9) Tolerance is a fruit of the spirit, to be shown through forgiving and enduring peace towards others. (193:2.2) With winsome tact and tolerance, the Master gracefully gains victory without engaging in unnecessary battle or destruction. (160:3.4) Family is where tolerance is learned. (84:7.28) A mature person looks upon all others with tenderness and tolerance. (160:1.6) As you mature and gain experience, practice tolerance with difficult individuals to cultivate tact and wisdom. (156:5.18) religious tolerance

name: tom-toms
description: Rhythm was once universal in early man's efforts to influence spirits, along with the belief that sweating could eliminate sickness. (90:4.6)

name: tombs
description: ancient times Jesus' times tombstone

name: tomorrow
description: Tomorrow is wholly in the hands of the Father. (130:5.3) Thomas asked Jesus if they should continue having everything in common, and the Master replied, “Be not unduly anxious for the morrow, sufficient for the day is the trouble thereof.” (140:6.13)

name: tongue
description: Believers should pray for the extension of the kingdom of heaven, for the human tongue is a member which few men can tame. (146:2.13) The secret of greatness lies in a great man's ability to subdue his own tongue, revealing the true measure of planetary greatness. (28:6.20)

name: tools
description: The Andonites developed sharp stone tools for hunting and survival. (63:1.2-3) (63:5.6) Domesticated animals were living tools that enabled man to advance from his primitive state to higher levels of civilization. (81:2.10) Improved tools and efficient machines determine the survival of contending groups in advancing civilization. (81:6.20) The invention of weapon tools enabled man to become a hunter, gaining freedom from food slavery and advancing civilization through ingenuity and discovery. (68:5.4) Old men and cripples, due to modification consequent upon age and disease, were set to work making tools and weapons, later transitioning to building irrigation works. (69:3.4)

name: torture
description: In ancient initiation ceremonies, self-torture and painful cutting were used to harden and impress youths. (70:7.10) The olden ideas of self-discipline embraced physical torture, with priests teaching the virtue of suffering through castration, and Hebrews, Hindus, and Buddhists devoutly following this doctrine of humiliation. (89:3.4)

name: totalitarianism
description: Materialism and mechanism may have been vanquished, but the devastating influence of twentieth-century secularism will continue to blight the spiritual experience of millions, born from the atheist science and totalitarian medieval Christian church. (195:8)

name: totems
description: Primitive tribes believed they were descended from animals of supposed ancestry, choosing them as their “totems”. (74:8.5) Totemism combines social and religious practices, with totems serving as both symbols of the group and their deity. (88:3.2)

name: traditions
description: The stability of growth in Edenic culture acts upon civilization with the compelling force of tradition, bridging the gulf between God and man. (51:6.5) Custom establishes institutions of survival, including industry, property, war for gain, and societal regulation through taboo, convention, and religious sanction, rooted in fear, ignorance, and superstition. (69:1.3) You are not obligated to accept outworn religious traditions, for true spiritual freedom and liberty come from following the truth wherever the spirit may lead you. (155:6.5-7) Traditions can be oppressive and enslaving, as seen in the burdens placed on the people by leaders who say one thing but do another. (175:1.8) Do not allow reverence for traditions to blind you from understanding the true spiritual realities of the kingdom. (157:2.2) Philosophy struggles for emancipation from dogma, tradition, and the bondage of superstition, while science fights for deliverance from the slavery of mathematics and the relative blindness of materialism. (12:9.5) It is pitiful to behold giant intellects in cultural bondage, stagnating at the level of logical intellectuality, held securely within the cruel grasp of tradition and authority. (101:7.4) Traditions provide stability and co-operation, but they also hinder initiative and creative freedom, reminiscent of the world's ongoing struggle with the dominance of outdated customs. (66:6.2) Religious traditions are the imperfectly preserved and unreliable records of the experiences of God-knowing men, altered by primitive man's tendency towards mythmaking. (4:5.1) Ritual sanctifies customs, creates myths, and is the product of myths, evolving from social to economic to religious ceremonial practices that can be personal, group, or both. (90:5.2) Tradition is a safe refuge for fearful, halfhearted souls who shun the struggles of the spirit and uncertainties of the mind, seeking an easy path devoid of faith voyages and daring adventures. (155:5.11) (191:5.3) Traditions sentimentally link the past with the present, but can also stifle initiative and creative powers. (66:6.2) Jesus

name: tranosta
description: Tranosta is the transcendental energy system linked exclusively to absonite beings, operating from the upper level of Paradise and known as such on Uversa. (42:2.18)

name: transcendence
description: The paradox of divine immanence and transcendence in religion challenges human comprehension, necessitating the function of theology to unify the concept of God's divine domination of the universe with the indwelling presence of God within each individual for intelligent worship and the hope of personality survival. (5:5.6) The mystery of God's transcendence should not lessen your faith, for He is personally present in a vast universe yet dwells within each of us. (12:7.12)

name: transcendental Almighty
description: This is a manifestation of God the Ultimate to the universes of time which is postulated to occur when God the Ultimate begins to function as supercontrol of the universes. At that time He will exercise supercontrol of supertime and transcended space over the administrative functions of the Supreme Almighty. (118:2.4)

name: Transcendental Recorders
description: Transcendental Recorders are personal gravity traversers, unlike local universe administrators. (23:3.2) Transcendental Recorders exist without discoverable form, unlike most beings in the seven superuniverses that possess tangible forms. (42:12.10)

name: Transcendentalers
description: Finaliters on Paradise strive to understand over one thousand groups of transcendental supercitizens, assisted by transcendental ministers, residing exclusively in the west of Paradise and playing a vital role in the superadministration of the master universe. (31:8.1-4) Absoniters, also known as Transcendentalers, exist beyond time and space, without beginnings or endings, eventuated rather than created, embodying the Ultimacy of Deity in transcendent acts throughout the master universe. (0:1.12) (55:4.7,19) The perfected mortal finaliters on Paradise strive to comprehend the nature and function of the transcendental supercitizens, aided by transcendental ministers, in the restricted area where the Transcendentalers exclusively reside. (31:8) Transcendentalers are solely focused on superadministering the master universe, beyond mortal ascent to Havona or the affairs of Havona and the superuniverses. (31:8.2) Transcendentalers are not involved in the superuniverses or Havona. (31:8.2) In Paradise classification, eventuated transcendental beings are loyal to the Paradise Trinity and obedient to the Ultimate, existing on four ultimate levels of personality activity and functioning on the seven levels of the absonite in twelve grand divisions. (30:1.92-93) In the next universe age, Transcendentalers may replace the finaliters as society's initiators of new supermaterial activities, possibly stepping in for the latter during the seventh epoch of evolutionary stability for outer space ministry. (55:4.7,19) Transcendentalers, ever loyal and eventuated, are neither creators nor creatures, existing on an absonite level beyond beginnings or endings, transcending time and space, and obedient to the Ultimate. (0:1.12) (30:1.92-93) (31:8.3) Upon reaching Paradise, mortal ascenders fraternize with upwards of three thousand different orders of Paradise Citizens, as well as with various groups of Transcendentalers and other unrevealed inhabitants. (27:2.3) The eventuation of transcendental beings on Paradise reveals their absonite nature as neither finite nor infinite, loyal to the Paradise Trinity, and functioning across various levels of personality activity and the absonite. (30:1.92-99) (31:8.3) (31:9.10) Solitary Messengers and Inspired Trinity Spirits maintain an organic relationship with Transcendentalers and the Master Architects. (31:9.13) The senior Architect of Master Universe, chief of Transcendentalers, administers the Trinity oath to mortal finaliters, marking their first serious contact with the governing corps of Paradise Transcendentalers. (31:8.4) (31:9.1) You can conceive of a Creator as a creature, yet struggle to comprehend the existence of the Transcendentalers, who create no beings, were never created, and are subject to God the Ultimate. (31:8.3) orders

name: transformation
description: You are transformed by Spirit of Truth, endowed with power to perform the perfect will of God. (143:2.4) Cures effected by Jesus for Elman’s patients resemble miracles, but are transformations in expectant and faith-dominated persons under the influence of a positive personality. (148:2.2) The supreme desire to do the Father's will, coupled with the joy of faith realization, leads to loving spiritual ministry, intellectual communion, and social service being a byproduct of spiritual transformation. (178:1.11)

name: transit trio
description: The transit trio assist Havona pilgrims in their final journey to attain the Universal Father and enter eternity. (26:9.2)

name: transition
description: Transition is always accompanied by confusion, and there will be little tranquillity in the religious world until the great struggle between the three contending philosophies of religion is ended. (99:4.9) The difficulties of religious transition create challenges but also opportunities for growth and harmony in human fellowship. (99:4) Forewarn all believers to traverse the fringe of conflict during the transition from earthly to spiritual life, embracing responsibilities and obligations with the light burden of truth. (159:3.7) (160:1.6) Great danger of breakdown during societal transition requires careful guidance from those well-versed in history and experience of social evolution. (81:6.40-41) Religion should stabilize ideals during dangerous times of transition to ensure a smooth evolution of civilization. (99:1.3-4) (99:2.6) A nation's intellectual level determines whether a mechanical age will liberate or devastate, as science guided by wisdom can successfully navigate the transition difficulties caused by unemployment from laborsaving machinery. (81:6.22)

name: transition ministers
description: Almost one half of the transition ministers on Urantia joined the Lucifer rebellion, but Van and other noble beings remained loyal defenders of Michael and his universe government. (67:3.2) The 6th order seraphim transition ministers assist in guiding beings from earthly life to morontia existence on the mansion worlds. (48:6.1) Almost ten percent of the transition ministers were ensnared in the Lucifer rebellion, as symbolized by John's vision of the great red dragon. (53:7.7) Morontia world seraphim minister tirelessly to aid creature transition from material to spiritual, guiding souls on the seven mansion worlds towards perfection and unity with the divine. (39:6) (48:6) Transition ministers are the seraphim closest to humans, often igniting the will-to-service through personal friendship. (48:6.34) Seraphim of transitional ministry, through seven orders of assignment, guide ascending souls on the mansion worlds towards perfection attainment and spiritual progression. (39:6.1) (48:6.4-37) # seraphic evangels # racial interpreters # mind planners # morontia counselors # technicians # recorder-teachers # ministering reserves Transition ministers in Jerusem circles provide guidance for souls moving through various stages of spiritual growth. (46:5.22)

name: translation
description: definition how it works examples

name: translation sleep
description: Morontia progressors enter translation sleep every ten days as they advance between mansion worlds on their journey. (47:3.10)

name: translators
description: Broadcast directors translate and adapt Ancients of Days broadcasts for local universes under general supervision. (44:4.9) Morontia Companion interpreters and translators are always available during your early mansonia career for any linguistic needs. (48:3.12-13) Translators are needed for people of different superuniverses to communicate effectively. (44:4.3)

name: transmigration
description: Ganid ceased to believe in soul transmigration after Jesus explained the limitations of animals' spiritual nature. (130:2.8) The stultifying belief in weary and monotonous lives led to a fear of the nonevolutionary perpetuation of self through reincarnation, robbing struggling mortals of hope and spiritual advancement. (94:2.3-6)

name: transport advisers
description: Seventy transport advisers on Urantia aid in routing and coordinating transportation on all inhabited planets. (44:5.6) Energy manipulator celestial artisans collaborate with star students in working out routings and assisting transport seraphim, serving as traffic supervisors on all inhabited planets, including Urantia with its corps of seventy transport advisers. (44:5.6)

name: transportation
description: Mobile subordinates of the Supreme Power Centers engage in a remarkable variety of autotransport, but they, like all space traversers, need assistance to overcome gravity when departing a sphere. (29:4.1) Improved transportation facilitates commerce, promoting the peace of Urantia through international trade organizations. (70:3.4) Transportation on Jerusem is facilitated by birds and vehicles, allowing material and morontia beings to travel at speeds ranging from two to five hundred miles per hour. (46:2.4) Spirit personalities proceed by liaison with spirit energy, enabling travel at a pace varying from two to five hundred miles per hour in the transportation system allied with circulatory streams of energy movement. (46:2.4) Fused mortals traverse space freely through Adjuster transit before being clothed with morontia forms in the resurrection halls of higher spheres. (49:6.19) (55:2.7)

name: transportation personalities
description: Transport personalities are seconaphim responsible for transporting pilgrims between superuniverses and Havona. (28:4.13)

name: travel
description: Visitors may travel to any realm they have experienced, hosted by celestial beings dedicated to entertaining superhuman students and other celestial visitors. (48:3.10) Travel between planets is facilitated by transport seraphim, who carry passengers to different worlds but never allow a mortal to return to their native planet during their temporal existence. (39:4.15) (112:3.7) (146:7.2) (150:3.5) The human desire for travel will be fully satisfied during the long journey to Paradise through the local, super-, and central universes. (30:3.12) Travel greatly furthers civilization and brotherhood through the multiplication of international social contacts, development of a common language, and interchange of people from different races and nations. (52:6.3) (81:6.25) Olden wars promoted travel and cultural intercourse, but modern methods of transport and communication now serve these ends more efficiently. (70:2.9)

name: treasure
description: Where your treasures are, there will your hearts be also. (140:6.11) (165:4.5) (165:5.4) Lay up your treasures where purses wax not old, for where your treasure is, there will your heart be also. (165:5.4) The parable of the treasure in the field teaches us that the kingdom of heaven is worth sacrificing everything for. (151:4.4)

name: treaties
description: Peace treaties were sealed through blood drinking and offerings of maidens, a tradition dating back to the earliest peace missions. (70:3.11) The first formal tribal treaty concerned intertribalizing a salt deposit, reflecting the historical significance of natural resources in early trade relations. (69:4.6) World peace cannot be maintained by treaties or any other type of makeshift juggling with the sovereignties of nationalism; world law enforced by world government is necessary. (134:6.9)

name: tree of life
description: Caligastia times Adam and Eve times tree worship

name: trees
description: During the Carboniferous period, trees grew to heights of 125 feet with trunks reaching a diameter of 7 feet, their spores spread by the wind. (59:5.22) Andon taught dispute settlement by beating, later Andonites settled disputes by ridicule and applause. (70:1.3) The beliefs of different tribes and races on tree spirits ranged from kindly to deceptive and cruel, with some cultures seeing most trees as indwelt by kind spirits while others viewed them with mistrust or as harboring cruel spirits. (85:2.4) Judge them by their fruits, for in my Father's eternal kingdom, the tree is known by its fruits. (140:3.19) (140:8.22) (142:5.2) (153:4.4) John's warning about trees not bringing forth good fruit being hewn down was a powerful message to those seeking baptism. (135:6.7) (166:4.9) Tree worship, one of the oldest religious practices, involved early marriages and embracing sturdy oaks for fertility, with differing beliefs in tree spirits among tribes and races worldwide. (85:2.3-5) for trees named in The Urantia Book see links below | apple; beeches; birches; breadfruit trees; citron; conifers; ferns; fig tree; magnolias; maples; oaks; palms; pines; redwoods; sycamores; tulip trees; walnuts

name: triad beliefs
description: The Trinity concept must not be confused with the triad beliefs derived from natural associations in human experience. (104:0.1-3)

name: triangles
description: The administrative triangle of Edentia's divisions is intricately woven into the celestial fabric of the universe. (43:1.7-9) The Jerusem administrative areas are directed from the one hundred triangles, clustered around the structures housing the local administration of Jerusem. (46:4.5) (46:8)

name: Triassic
description: The Triassic period, an early reptilian age from 150 to 125 million years ago, saw extensive erosion deposits, arid climates, and the rapid evolution of reptilian dinosaurs. (60:1)

name: triata
description: Triata is threefold organizations of energy-matter responding to linear gravity in Havona, the superpower mother systems of time and space creations. (11:8.7) The triune existential energy of Havona ensures perfect balance and harmony within the immense worlds of triata physical constitution, utilizing antigravity to organize both material functions and spiritual activities. (14:3.6) (42:2.16-17)

name: tribal mark
description: Cain bore no tribal mark, seeking spiritual guidance and gaining superiority through divine assistance. (76:2.8) Tribal marks were cut on boys as part of their intensive puberty initiation, designed to harden and prepare them for life's challenges. (70:7.10) Survival hinges on bearing a tribal mark, proof of group affiliation and protection against harm since ancient times. (68:1.2)

name: tribes
description: Tribes often subvert the sovereignty of the territorial state, hindering the evolution of political sovereignty towards all mankind. (134:5.8) Tribal war chiefs established patrilineal leadership while clan peace chiefs ruled through the mother line, fostering local self-government but hindering the growth of unified nations.
