(70:4.10) Originally a loose confederation of clans, the native Australians never developed a tribal form of government. (70:4.9) about tribes and racial groups named in The Urantia Book see links below | aborigines (Australian); aborigines (Indian); Adamites; Adamsonites; Amadonites; Ammonites; Amorites; Andites; Andonites; Anglo-Saxons; Arabs; Aryans; Assyria; Babylonians; Badonan tribes; Baganda tribes; Basques; Bedouins; Berbers; Blackfoot Indians; blue race; Borneans; Bretons; brown race; Bushmen; Calebites; Canaanites; Caucasians; Chaldeans; Cro-Magnons; Cuthites; Cutites; Danubians; Dravidians; Dyaks; Edenites; Edomites; Elamites; Eskimos; Ezraeon; Finns; Foxhall peoples; Gad; Gibeonites; Gileadites; Greeks; green race; Guites; Hamathites; Heidelberg race; Hittites; Incas; India:racial groups; indigo race; Iroquois; Jebusites; Jews; Kateri; Kenites; Levites; Malaysia; Melanesians; Mesopotamia; Midianites; Moabites; Mongoloids; Moqui tribe; Neanderthalers; Negroids; Nerites; Nodites; orange race; Parsees; Patagonians; Persians; Philistines; Phoenicians; Polynesia; Pygmies; red race; Romans; Ruben; Saharans; Samaritans; Sangiks; Scandinavians; Semites; Sepharvites; Sethites; Siberia; Suites; Sumerians; Suntites; Thrace; Toda tribe; Turanians; Vanites; white race; yellow race; Zulus

name: tribute money
description: The words of Jesus were: “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's and render to God the things that are God's.” (174:2.2)

name: trilobites
description: Trilobites, the dominant living creatures, were sexed animals, some with aborted eyes, that existed in many forms and included mud eaters. (59:1.19) The trilobites were the predecessors of crustaceans but nearly disappeared, giving way to larger mollusks and cephalopods in dominating the seas. (59:2.10) (59:3.5,11) (59:5.8)

name: Trimurti
description: The Trimurti, supreme trinity of Hinduism, consists of Brahma, Vishnu, and Siva, with Brahma being self-created and identified with fate, while Vishnu periodically incarnates and becomes real in the imagination of Indians, and Siva is lord of life and death as well as destruction and fertility. (94:4.4-5)

name: Trinities
description: Trinities are always deity reality, never personality reality, encompassing and correlating personal aspects within a collective sense. (106:5.3-4) experiential Trinities: * 1st | Trinity Ultimate * 2nd | Trinity Absolute The Universal I AM is the primal cause and unqualified source of both the Trinity and the Absolute, culminating in the personalization of the Deity Absolute. (56:9.5) The ultimate Trinities include creature personalities to achieve true infinity. (106:3.3) Trinities are truths of relationship and facts of co-ordinate Deity manifestation, encompassing Deity realities seeking realization and personalization, culminating in God the Supreme, God the Ultimate, and God the Absolute as divine inevitabilities. (0:12.4)

name: trinitization
description: Trinitization may accomplish what is otherwise nonattainable, leading to the potential union of the Supreme-Ultimate and the factualization of God the Absolute. (0:12.8) by creatures by Deities

name: Trinitized Ambassadors
description: Trinitized Ambassadors, numbering 3.5 billion, are Trinity-embraced ascendant mortals of Son- or Spirit-fused nature, serving mostly in the minor sectors of the superuniverse. (18:4.5) (22:6.1) Trinitized Ambassadors serve as emissaries of Ancients of Days on minor sectors within the superuniverse. (18:4.5) (22:6.3) In Uversa personality register, Trinitized Ambassadors are recorded. (30:2.59) Trinitized Ambassadors serve on Spirit- and Son-fusion worlds, representing superior minds selected for the Trinity embrace to assist superuniverse rulers in understanding and administering the interests of their respective groups. (22:6.2) Trinitized Ambassadors, the second order of Trinitized Sons of Selection, are recruited from Spirit- and Son-fused ascendant creatures who have reached Havona and attained Paradise, then trinitized in classes of seven thousand and commissioned as ambassadors in the superuniverses. (22:6.1)

name: Trinitized Custodians
description: Assisted by several billion seconaphim, the Trinitized Custodians serve faithfully in groups of seventy thousand, with one seventh of each group dedicated to a specific superuniverse. (22:5.5) Trinitized Custodians administer group affairs and foster collective projects, dealing solely with groups, not individuals. (22:5.6) Trinitized Custodians, embraced by Trinity, serve in the courts of all three divisions, from the Ancients of Days to the Recents of Days. (18:4.5) (22:5.1-2) Trinitized Custodians in Uversa personality register. (30:2.58) The Trinitized Custodians, numbering 70 million, serve as officers of the superuniverse major and minor sector governments, overseeing collective projects and acting as custodians of various undertakings in the three divisions. (18:4.5) (22:5.5,6) Trinitized Custodians are recognized for valiantly cooperating with subsequently trinitized mortals. (22:5.3) The Trinitized Sons of Selection, recruited from evolutionary seraphim, midway creatures, Spirit-fused, and Son-fused mortals who have traversed Havona and ascended to Paradise, are embraced by the Trinitized Custodians and Ambassadors before being assigned to the courts of the Ancients of Days after training in Havona. (22:1.11)

name: Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy
description: The highest order of the Stationary Sons of the Trinity, the Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy, direct the sacred spheres nearest Paradise, each presided over by ten supreme administrators. (13:1.2-3) (13:2.9-10) (18:1.1) The Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy reveal the intertwined secrets of the Supreme, Ultimate, and Supreme-Ultimate. (13:2.10) The Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy collectively know all secrets, directing Ascendington and other worlds of the Father, little comprehended by other orders. (13:2.9) The Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy are fully known only by their special world groups, little comprehended by other orders, and ardently loved by those who attain Paradise. (13:2.9) The Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy oversee the Paradise worlds of the Father, the highest order of Stationary Sons of Trinity. (13:1.2) The Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy are inscribed in personality registers. (30:1.8) (30:2.23) 70 Trinitized Secrets of Supremacy oversee the seven innermost worlds in the personal circuit of the Universal Father. (18:1.1)

name: Trinitized Sons of Attainment
description: The Trinitized Sons of Attainment, including the Mighty Messengers, Those High in Authority, and Those without Name and Number, were ascendant mortals who attained Paradise, were embraced by the Paradise Trinity, and subsequently assigned to superuniverse service. (15:10.12) (22:1.10) composition functions

name: trinitized sons of destiny
description: Trinitized Sons of Destiny, beings produced by finaliters and Paradise-Havoners, embody aspects of the unrevealed master universe function of the Supreme-Ultimate. (22:7.14) In Uversa personality register, the Trinitized Sons of Destiny and the Corps of Unrevealed Sons of Destiny are listed. (30:2.64,138)

name: Trinitized Sons of God
description: The Melchizedek receivers advised after rebellion, governing Urantia until Adam's arrival. (67:6.5) Completion seraphim are attached to the high trinitized beings of the universes, serving in light and life ministries. (39:9.1) Trinity-embraced sons of God are constantly increasing in numbers, never having strayed or been lost in the service of the superuniverse governments. (22:1.13) Trinitized Sons of God function as self-governing groups across the superuniverses, serving under the direction of the Ancients of Days. (22:1.14) Trinitized Sons of God have their sole homes on Paradise, near the Most Holy Sphere. (13:2.4) Trinitized Sons of God are classified as: trinitized sons of attainment, selection, perfection, and creature-trinitized sons, all in personality registers. (30:1.25-28) (30:2.54) None of the Trinitized Sons have ever gone astray, remaining steadfast in their service to the superuniverse governments. (22:1.13) (53:7.4) Trinitized Sons of God serve as Most High Assistants in harmonizing Nebadon with the ideals of Paradise. (37:4.2) Trinitized Sons of God serve on commissions certifying Havona pilgrims for Deity adventure. (26:7.3) types

name: Trinitized Sons of Perfection
description: The affectionate, loyal, humble, and supremely wise Trinitized Sons of Perfection would greatly benefit Uversa with their value to superuniverse administrations, encouraging all beings to attempt trinitization after contributing essential experiential realities. (22:10.6-7) Trinitized Sons of Perfection are limited in experience due to time-space inhibition, ensuring their reserve status for future universe epochs. (22:9.7) Creature-trinitized sons of perfection, comprised of perfected humans and Paradise-Havona personalities, are embraced by the Paradise Trinity in classes of seven thousand, serving as valuable adjuncts to the superuniverse governments. (22:9-10) Each Trinitized Son of Perfection personifies a single universal ideal, embodying the very wisdom of the divine Trinity. (22:10.2-7) Trinitized Sons of Perfection are held in reserve for a future universe epoch due to their time-space inhibited experiential capacity. (22:9.7) In Paradise classification, they are in the group 9. Trinitized Sons of Perfection. (30:1.27) Trinitized Sons of Perfection, lacking experience and sometimes erring in judgment, are less reliable than their ascendant associates, yet are held in reserve for future experiential function. (22:1.13) (22:9.6-7) Twice-trinitized Sons of Perfection make ascenders grateful for their lowly origin and capacity for experience, despite lacking the profound personal experience of their ascendant counterparts. (22:9.6-8) Trinitized Sons of Perfection remain in their superuniverse of assignment, while their trinitized associates travel throughout the grand universe under the direction of the Ancients of Days. (22:1.14) (22:10.5) The Trinitized Sons of Perfection may be solely acted upon by the embrace of the Paradise Trinity, leaving them otherwise unchanged. (22:9.5) Trinitized Sons of Perfection train under Supreme Executives and Trinity Teacher Sons, but are limited by time-space inhibition, necessitating their reserve for future experiential function. (22:9.7) The Trinitized Sons of Perfection serve as Celestial Guardians and High Son Assistants to the Ancients of Days. (22:1.12) (22:8.4)

(0:12.7) The Trinity Absolute, comprising God the Supreme, God the Ultimate, and the unrevealed Consummator of Universe Destiny, represents the ultimate unification of personal, superpersonal, and nonpersonal levels towards the experientialization of Absolute Deity. (0:12.7) (31:9.14) (106:5.1) (106:8.11) The Trinity Absolute could only be fully realized after the entire master universe had evolved to completion. (106:5.1) The Trinity Absolute represents the final expression of infinity, the absolute actualization of all potentials, and the ultimate culmination of transcendental and evolutionary-experiential realities. (106:5.1) (106:6.3) The full function of the Trinity Absolute transcends the space-stage of the master universe. (106:6.2) The actualization of God the Absolute is in consequence of the unification of the Absolute Trinity, leading to the experiential realization of absolute divinity and the unification of absolute meanings on absolute levels. name: Trinity of Trinities
description: The Trinity of Trinities completes the cycle of reality through unlimited integration. (106:8.12) The Universal Absolute is the impersonal presence of the unified Trinity of Trinities, manifesting as the primal cause and unqualified source of both the Trinity and Absolute. (56:9.5) The Trinity of Trinities is the ultimate culmination of all that is implied in the fifteen triunities and associated triodities, where the experiential infinite attains identity with the existential infinite. (106:8.1) The Trinity of Trinities is the ultimate culmination of all triunities and triodities, embodying the unity of experiential and existential infinity in the pre-experiential I AM. (15:10.11) Trinity-origin beings in personality registers express the diverse forms of divine creation. (30:1.18-23) (30:2.22) Trinity-origin beings have transit prerogatives allowing them to reach any point in Orvonton from Uversa in less than one year. (19:7.4) We intercommunicate instantaneously with all Trinity-origin beings except the Inspired Spirits. (19:7.5) Trinity-origin beings, brought into existence by the Paradise Trinity, are all freewill endowed and none have ever gone astray. (19:7.3) types

name: Trinity Teacher Sons
description: With 21 billion in service, the numbers of Trinity Teacher Sons are constantly increasing within the Paradise reserves. (19:1.2) (20:7.2) In Nebadon, there are between 15,000 to 20,000 Trinity Teacher Sons, excluding creature-trinitized assistants. (35:0.1) The Trinity Teacher Sons, created by Trinity, serve as supernal teachers across the grand universe to all personalities, human and divine. (7:6.6) (19:1.3) (20:1.11) (20:7.1) (20:10.3) Creature-trinitized sons are apprentices of the Teacher Sons, assisting in the spiritual and intellectual advancement of beings across the local creations. (20:8.2) (22:1.12) (22:8.2-4) (22:9.1) (55:4.17) The Teacher Sons, who do not incarnate, communicate with visitation worlds through the assistance of Brilliant Evening Stars. (20:9.3) (37:2.2,9) Multiple orders of Teacher Sons come in groups to the spiritualizing worlds, remaining for at least one thousand years to facilitate the transition to the era of light and life. (52:7.2) Trinity Teacher Sons in personality registers guide Daynals. (30:1.19) (30:2.30,41) Trinity Teacher Sons initiate spiritual ages while Magisterial Sons terminate dispensations. (20:9.2) (52:7.8) Throughout the seven years of his assignment, the Trinity Teacher Son remained unsure of the true identity of his seraphic associate, knowing that their beloved Sovereign could be disguised as any seraphim. (119:4.4) Trinity Teacher Sons rely on the spiritual influence of Creator and Magisterial Sons, lacking personal spiritual drawing power. (20:7.5) Trinity Teacher Sons go straight to the superuniverse headquarters without preliminary training in the central universe, utilizing the spiritual influence of Creator and Magisterial Sons in their ministry to local universes. (20:7.5) All descending Sons of God have divine origins, dedicated to the ministry of service on worlds and systems of time and space, facilitating the progress of lowly creatures in their climb to Paradise, including the order of Trinity Teacher Sons. (20:1) (20:7.1) The Trinity Teacher Sons in the second circle of residence in Jerusem circles represent the personal representatives of the Universal Father, overseeing the training of primary Teacher Sons with assistance from Brilliant Evening Stars and creature-trinitized sons. (46:5.13) service

name: Trinity Ultimate
description: The mobilization of the seven finaliter corps by the Ultimate Trinity signifies the gathering together of potentials, personalities, minds, spirits, absonites, and experiential actualities for inconceivable developments in outer space. (31:10.10) The Trinity Ultimate actively co-ordinates the transcendental in eternal unity. (106:4.1) beings that comprise how is defined goals

name: triodities
description: Triodities are directly concerned with the cosmic appearance of experiential Deities such as the Supreme, Ultimate, and Absolute, while triunities focus on the functional unification of infinity. (104:5.12) The existential triodities encompass all absolute actuality and all infinite potentiality, originating from the Paradise Trinity. (115:4.1) The growth of Supremacy derives from the triodities, Trinity, Almighty, and the Conjoint Actor, leading to a dual motion intensively towards Paradise and extensively towards the Absolutes. (115:4.7) (115:6) Infinity is transmitively associated in triodities, allowing for new cosmic experientials despite its eternal presence in the seven Absolutes. (115:2.1) Non-Father triune relationships, known as triodities, are distinct from Father triunities and involve interrelationships among absolute actuals and potentials that contribute to the functional unification of infinity and the emergence of experiential Deities. (104:5) The Supreme, divine channel of triodities, guides the cosmic evolution towards unity with Paradise Deity. (115:6) (117:3.2) triodities of actuality | Absolutes:of actuality triodities of potentiality | Absolutes:the three

name: triune-origin beings
description: Triune-origin beings are creations of the three Paradise Deities, including the Trinity, and the Trinitized Corps. (30:1.2) Living beings on Paradise are classified based on their inherent and attained relationship to the Paradise Deities, including those of triune origin, dual origin, and single origin. (30:1.1-23) Master Spirit Number Seven is adviser to all triune-origin beings, directing both the ascending pilgrims of Havona and the presiding Spirit of the seventh superuniverse. (16:3.14) types

name: triunities
description: 1st triunity 2nd triunity 3rd triunity 4th triunity 5th triunity 6th triunity 7th triunity other how is defined functions

name: trivialities
description: Avoid loss of time through trivialities and focus wholeheartedly on preaching the gospel. (163:4.5) Jesus wasted little time on trifles, focusing instead on the essentials of his work as a painstaking craftsman. (134:9.7) Many of you focus on trivialities, neglecting the essential realities of attuning to your divine Adjuster and achieving an understanding consecration to eternal aims. (110:3.4)

name: Troas
description: From Athens they journeyed to Ephesus by way of Troas, discussing the worship of objects crafted by human hands along the way. (130:0.3) (133:6.1)

name: tropics
description: Races seldom advanced who sought ease in tropical climates; progress thrived in the open lands and higher latitudes where cold and hunger fueled action and invention. (64:1.3) (81:6.7)

name: troposphere
description: The troposphere, the lower 5 or 6 miles of atmosphere, is where weather phenomena occur, with the temperature steadily falling for six or eight miles before reaching a constant range in the stratosphere. (58:2.6)

name: trumpets
description: Religious leaders must stop trying to call modern man to spiritual battle with olden trumpet blasts and instead, adopt new and up-to-date slogans for meaningful progress. (195:6.10) The wealthy Pharisees, devoted to almsgiving, would blow trumpets before giving charity, even opening their doors to street beggars during banquets. (147:5.2)

name: trust
description: Trust is a new human acquisition brought about by planetary helper seraphim, inculcating faith into the minds of evolving men. (39:5.7) Trust and faith, the cardinal precepts of Melchizedek teachings, were the foundation of his third revelation of truth on Urantia. (92:4.7) Childlike trust secures entrance into the kingdom of heavenly ascent, where the Thought Adjuster translates man's primitive sense of duty into a higher faith in eternal realities of revelation, leading to supreme attainment through the divine will. (102:1.1) Trust is the solemn measure of character and self-mastery, a crucial test of will creatures. (28:6.13) The reliability of personalities in a universe is determined by their closeness to Deity, and as they move further away in origin, an escalation in discord, disorder, and occasional defiance can occur. (32:3.7) Faith produces a sublime trust in the goodness of God even in the face of bitter disappointment and crushing defeat. (101:3.6) The Universal Father freely trusts us with part of himself, inculcating trust into our minds through the ministry of planetary seraphim. (39:5.7) Trust in the fruits of the spirit leads to enduring peace and merciful ministry. (193:2.2) Judas refused to exercise trust, leading to his departure from Jesus and the group. (193:3.2) Advancing individuals by augmenting trusts gradually, based on character development, prevents disaster, while prematurely placing responsibility courts failure. (28:6.15) Trustworthiness is the sacred and solemn measure of character, revealing the true mastery of self. (28:6.13)

name: truth
description: definition historically function of truth seek of truth embrace truth what believers should do warnings about truth Spirit of Truth | Spirit of Truth

name: truth, beauty, and goodness
description: Truth, beauty, and goodness are combined in a single divine ideal, unified in the power of Supremacy and personalized as a God of paternal love. (56:10.15) Divine love, mercy, and ministry are the meaningful values ​​of reality revealed by the Father, the Son, and the Spirit, which combine in harmony to represent loving mercy to all creatures. (56:10.19) Truth, beauty, and goodness in harmony reveal full divinity to universe mortals. (56:10.20) In their embrace, man is glorified to live and do righteousness, transcending the limitations of the material world through divine love. (196:3.32) Health, sanity, and happiness are integrations of truth, beauty, and goodness in human experience, achieved through the unification of energy systems, idea systems, and spiritual systems. (2:7.11) Love embraces truth, beauty, and goodness, as Jesus revealed. (5:4.6) The relationship between reason, wisdom, and faith leads to the unity of mind, soul, and spirit in the pursuit of truth, beauty, and goodness. (103:9.10) The sum of truth, beauty, and goodness is love, the manifestation of God as the spiritual Father in the loving ministry of the Infinite Spirit and the special ministry of the Sons of Paradise, who reflect the Isle of Paradise in the material creation. (56:10.17,19) The unity of truth, beauty, and goodness is realized only in spiritual experience. (44:7.2-4) (196:3.23-24)

name: Truth-Justice-Righteousness
description: The pre-Salemite Egyptian triad preached truth, justice, and righteousness to their followers in the Nile valley thousands of years before the Hebrew scriptures were written. (95:3.3)

name: Tubal-Cain
description: Lamech's exultation over weapons of Tubal-Cain inspired the concept of mercy and forbearance illustrated by the term seventy-seven in the Scriptures. (159:1.7)

name: tulip trees
description: Tulip trees emerged alongside fig trees and magnolias 90,000,000 years ago, as angiosperms overran continents and land plants dominated Europe and western North America. (60:3.7)

name: tunic
description: Jesus silently see how the soldiers divided his clothes, casting lots for the seamless tunic he had, that reached down to near the knees. (187:2.8)

name: Turanians
description: The Andites upstepped the ancestors of the Turanians, influencing the development of these peoples in central Asia and beyond. (78:5.6) The Turanians possess a significant Caucasoid inheritance due to their Andite blend and Andonic crossing. (81:4.11) Traces of Andite blood persist among the Turanian and Tibetan peoples, as evidenced by blond types found in these regions. (79:1.8)

name: turban
description: Jesus wore a turban that was divided among the soldiers. (187:2.8)

name: Turkestan
description: Adamites and Nodites entered the then fertile regions of Turkestan, blending with the superior inhabitants and extending the Andite race northward. (78:4.2) (80:2.5) (81:1.2) The Andites of Turkestan were instrumental in the spread of civilization across Eurasia and influenced the development of many ancient cultures. (78:6.3) (79:1) (79:6.13) The Andonites of Turkestan maintained five or six settlements in the north and east, with descendants scattered throughout the region and persisted in isolated islands throughout Eurasia. (78:1.5) (78:3.5) (80:8.2) Urban life and commerce thrived in Turkestan, where cities emerged as central hubs for trade and manufacturing activities. (79:1.4) The Aryan conquerors of India, originating from Turkestan, marked the final exodus of the Andites during the second penetration of India. (79:4.1) The barbarians of Turkestan, with their superior cavalry and tamed horses, overran Mesopotamia and Europe, leading to a significant cultural setback and the assimilation of the remnants of Mesopotamian civilization. (78:8.3-5) (80:9.6-7) Turkestan saw a bronze culture flourishing before 9000 B.C., marking an era of early industrial and trading cities where the Andites excelled in working with iron, gold, and copper. (81:3.4-5) The climatic destruction of rich grazing grounds in Turkestan around 12,000 B.C. led to the development of new industries and manufacturing practices among the tribes of the region. (79:7.5) (81:3.1) Turkestan's civilization was constantly rejuvenated by Andite arrivals, shaping the early Aryan mother tongue that influenced modern languages worldwide. (78:5.3) The cradle of civilization in southwestern Asia, from the Nile to Turkestan, was established due to climate. (81:1.1) The mother cult of Eve thrived in ancient Crete, persisting through the ages and later being incorporated into early Christian worship with Mary. (80:7.7) The fertile open hunting area in Turkestan played a crucial role in the evolution of a superior culture in southwestern Asia. (81:6.4) The ancient Andites of Turkestan were pioneers in horse domestication, contributing to the lasting influence of their culture. (81:2.12) The first wooden homes were built in Turkestan by the peoples of the highlands, resembling the early log cabins of American pioneers. (81:2.16) The increasing aridity of Turkestan forced Andites to invent methods due to the natural circumstances. (81:6.3) The virile and vigorous inhabitants of Turkestan were absorbed by the northward-moving Adamites, leading to progress in civilization, art, science, and social culture. (78:3.4) The overpopulation in Turkestan in 15,000 B.C. drove the first extensive Andite movement toward India. (78:3.5) (79:2.4) The vestiges of Adamsonite headquarters in the foothills of Turkestan mark the historical center of civilization in the region east of the Caspian Sea. (77:5.10) The yellow race drove Andonites into Turkestan after clearing them from eastern Asia and Siberia. (79:6.1)

name: turtles
description: Turtles, descendants of ancient reptiles, thrived and evolved over millions of years alongside other members of the saurian family. (60:2.13) (60:3.14,20) (61:2.6) (65:2.10)

name: Tut
description: Tut and entire council lost in rebellion, while Van and his court remained loyal, Ang and three food council members survived, Fad and five from education faculty saved, and Hap and the college of revealed religion stayed loyal with Van. (67:4.1) Tut led the governors of advanced tribal relations, promoting peaceful group associations and improving tribal governments. (66:5.28-30)

name: Tutankhamen
description: Tutankhamen, son-in-law of Ikhnaton, reverted to old worship, leading to the priests gaining power and eventually seizing the crown. (95:5.10)

name: twelvefold adjutants of the Havona circuits
twentieth century
description: During the psychologically unsettled times of the twentieth century, thousands became humanly dislocated, in need of the stabilization of sound religion amidst economic upheavals and moral crosscurrents. (99:4.6)

name: twins
description: In the treetops, mid-mammal ancestors birthed handsome twins, male and female, adapted to a warm climate with minimal body hair. (62:3.1) No two snow crystals are exactly alike, just as no two twins are alike. (111:4.6) The twin Primate ancestors quickly became the next vital step in prehuman evolution, standing upright from birth and communicating in new symbols. (62:3.10) (62:4.1) Primitive tribes often killed deformed or sickly children, and twins were believed to be caused by magic or infidelity, resulting in one usually being killed. (68:6.8) Twins were once regarded as very lucky or very unlucky, as early man feared and revered all unusual persons and beings. (68:6.8) (85:6.2) In the treetop abode of simian ancestors, twins were born - a male and a female, who were handsome and lived comfortably in a warm climate. (62:3.1)

name: two-and-two
description: Union of souls is essential for the mobilization of wisdom, as exemplified in the 70 evangelists sent out in pairs by the Master to spread the gospel. (160:2.7) (163:1.3) (181:2.12) Jesus sent out the apostles two-and-two to teach the glad tidings of the kingdom and acquire practical experience in dealing personally with their fellow men. (138:1.1) (193:3.2)

name: two-brained mortals
description: Urantians, as two-brained mortals, exhibit a unique combination of imagination, adventure, and philosophy, but lack the spiritual and ethical depth of the three-brained orders. (49:5.13-18)

name: two sons
description: The parable of two sons taught by Jesus revealed the true nature of obedience and faithfulness. (173:3)

name: Tyrannus
description: Paul taught in the school of Tyrannus, where he laid the foundation for Christianity in Ephesus during his two-year residence. (133:6.3)

name: Tyre
description: The rich widow of Tyre offered money for healing, seeking only physical cures and not the gospel of the kingdom. (149:1.9) At the tomb of Hiram near Tyre, the apostles and evangelists preached and baptized a polyglot population from July 11 to July 24. (156:4.1) In these days, Joseph of Tyre housed Jesus and the apostles as they taught and preached throughout the city and its environs. (156:4.1) A Christian church was built on the site of the Melkarth temple in Tyre, where Jesus taught the believers about the Father's love. (156:4.2) Tyre and Sidon would have repented in sackcloth and ashes. (163:6.5) Jesus' 18th morontia appearance was at Tyre, bringing the message of salvation through bearing the fruits of the divine spirit. (193:2)

name: Tyrian purple
description: The leaders of Tyrian purple manufacture, believing in the kingdom, spread the message of fatherhood of God and brotherhood of man worldwide. (156:4.3)

name: Tyrus
description: It was one of the towns visited during the Perea mission of the seventy, between January 3, 30 until the death of Jesus (165:0.1)

name: ugliness
description: Materialism and atheism are the maximation of ugliness, contrasting with the unifying beauty of harmonious reality. (56:10.4) The stimulative surroundings of relative goodness and beauty stimulate the irrepressible reach for better things in the struggle for idealism. (3:5.11)

name: Ukraine
description: The Andonites entered Europe via the Balkans and Ukraine after being pushed northward by the Andites for thousands of years. (80:9.6)

name: ultimacy
description: The comprehension, realization, and realization of God's spirit may lead to the ultimate realization of God's ultimacy in evolving mortals. (5:5.11)

name: ultimata
description: Emergent energy, also known as ultimata, transitions through puissant and gravity stages, responding to Paradise gravity and Universe Power Directors, ultimately becoming the active ancestor of all universe matter. (0:6.6) (11:7.8) (16:4.12) (42:2.10-13) becomes responsive to linear gravity, Universe Power Directors When fully liberated from the Unqualified Absolute, emergent energy becomes responsive to linear gravity and the Universe Power Directors. (16:4.12) Emergent energies pass through distinct phases before appearing as universe power, comprised of puissant energy and gravity energy. (42:2.10-13) Emergent energy, responsive to Paradise gravity, directs force-energies at the pre-electronic level, resembling a giant heart pulsing towards the outermost borders of physical space. (0:6.6) (11:5.5) (11:8.6) Cosmic force and emergent energy universally follow curved space paths of lessened resistance surrounding the Isle of Paradise. (11:7.8) Ultimatonic rays represent the initial activity of ultimatons as they veer towards electronic organization. (42:5.3-4) Ultimata, originally neutral, evolve to exhibit positive and negative qualities as emergent energy systems responsive to Paradise gravity undergo metamorphosis. (11:8.6) Emergent energy at the pre-electronic level remains unresponsive to linear gravity while still being receptive to Paradise gravity. (0:6.6)

name: ultimatons
description: In each cubic inch of the most nearly empty space in Nebadon, there are about one hundred ultimatons, equivalent to one electron, held together by mutual attraction. (42:3.3) (42:4.6) (42:6.4) Force organizers and power directors play a crucial role in accelerating ultimatons back to puissant energy, allowing them to escape the universal space drift and achieve deindividuation. (42:6.3) Cold and other influences creatively organize ultimatons into electrons throughout space. (42:4.5,7) The critical level of condensation causes explosive ultimatonic energy pressure in dying stars on the verge of mass explosion. (41:3.6) (41:7.15) Energy is stored in electrons as ultimatons slow down and aggregate into matter. (42:5.4) Ultimatons are the prime physical units of material existence, energy particles that make up electrons and cannot be converted back into puissant energy by solar heat or pressure. (42:3.3) (42:4.8) Ultimatons escape physical existence only in the terminal disruption of suns, functioning by mutual attraction within the circular Paradise-gravity pull. (42:6.3) Ultimatons demonstrate mutual resistance to absoluta and undergo multiple physical phases before achieving electronic organization. (42:6.4) Ultimatons, the first measurable form of energy, originate from Paradise within the Universal Father, demonstrating the inherent connection between matter and energy in the universe. (42:1.2) Ultimatons, the first measurable form of energy, have Paradise as their nucleus, serving as the source of all physical phenomena in the universe. (42:1.2) The ultimatonic leakage from suns initiates the adventure of electronic association and energy materialization in space. (41:9.1) Morontia material, containing one hundred physical elements and one hundred forms of energy organization, is created by modifying primary units of matter on architectural spheres with double the elements of evolved planets. (48:1.3) The cosmic ancestry of ultimatons remains a mysterious phenomenon, originating in the force-charge of space and forever connected to the grand outlines of Paradise. (15:4.1) Primary associators manipulate atoms, electrons, and ultimatons in compliance with universal law, releasing accumulated energies during seasons of minus manifestations, a technique beyond our understanding of energy and matter. (29:4.33) Ultimatons proceed in direct lines through space, forming wave phenomena when subjected to certain observations. (42:5.14) Ultimatons respond to Paradise gravity and mutual attraction, not to linear gravity when unassociated, unlike atoms and electrons subject to local gravity. (41:9.2) (42:4.3) (42:6.2-6) Ultimatons, under certain conditions, can achieve revolutionary velocity and partial antigravity in the never-ending metamorphosis of energy and matter. (42:4.10) (42:6.3,4) Solar heat and pressure lack the ability to transform ultimatons back into puissant energy, as ultimatons are held in universal space drift by mutual attraction to Paradise-gravity. (41:7.12) (42:4.8) (42:6.3) Ultimatons spread or cluster in accordance with their axial revolutionary velocities, determining electronic dimensions and reactions. (42:6.4,6) (42:7.3,10) Supreme Power Centers skillfully transmute ultimatons into electrons to control and direct energy at the electronic stage, but are limited by the drawing power of linear gravity at the atomic level. (42:4.3)

name: ultraviolet rays
description: The ultraviolet rays of sunlight and various mechanical productions may cause harm. (42:5.9)

name: Umajor the fifth
description: Seventy study worlds rotate around Umajor the fifth, dedicated to intellectual training and development for the progression towards eternity. (15:13.3) Umajor the fifth, the headquarters of Splandon, is where the seventy spheres of intellectual training for the superuniverse converge. (15:7.9) (15:13.3) (15:14.7)

name: umbilical cord
description: The preserved umbilical cord, set with pearls, was mankind's first prized fetish, considered a powerful object of great value in Africa. (88:1.8)

name: Uminor the third
description: Uminor the third, the headquarters of your minor sector Ensa, is situated amidst the seven spheres of higher physical studies within the ascendant life. (15:7.8) (15:14.7)

name: unbelievers
description: Religious traits can stem from nonreligious sources, but without spiritual roots, they lack true survival value and spiritual significance. (102:7.4) Believers’ certain reply to unbelievers: How do you know that I do not know? (102:6.5) (102:7.6-9) Morality, loyalty, filial piety, honesty, and idealism can stem from nonreligious beliefs, but only spiritual roots can yield true spiritual growth and survival value. (102:7.4) Challenge unbelievers with profound faith and unshakable certainty. (103:8.5) Unbelievers may show religious loyalty to their espoused cause, reflecting the unquestioning loyalty and wholehearted devotion found in all religions. (100:6.1) Jesus does not offer the same peace to unbelievers as he does to his followers, who find solace in doing the will of God rather than seeking joy and satisfaction in material world. (181:1.7) It would be far better for disciples to placate the wrath of unbelievers in ordinary circumstances, to continue preaching the gospel and live on the earth for many years. (181:2.15)

name: uncertainty
description: In the 23rd Psalm reference, it is acknowledged that one may face uncertainty in life. (48:6.12) Uncertainty, confusion, and doubt do not hinder survival; only resistance to the divine Monitor's guidance can prevent the evolving soul's ultimate destiny. (110:3.5) Early man lived in uncertainty and constant fear of chance, making life an exciting game of gamble where existence was the main interest and supreme fascination. (86:1.4) Face difficulties and uncertainties without fear, rise indomitably, maintain a well-balanced poise throughout every effort. (48:6.35) Ascendant pilgrims feast upon uncertainty, fattening upon disappointment, enthralling over defeat, invigorating in the face of difficulties, exhibiting indomitable courage, and exercising unconquerable faith. (26:5.3) in predictions Embracing uncertainty

name: Unclassified Centers
description: The unclassified centers are recorded in the Uversa personality register. (30:2.99) Supreme Power Centers act as balance wheels on spheres of extraordinary energy relationships, serving as universe balance wheels or energy governors in spheres of the most extraordinary energy relationships. (29:2.19)

name: Uncle Joshua
